text.NRSV: Paul, called to be an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and our brother Sosthenes,
text.Rotherham: Paul, a called apostle of Jesus Christ, through God’s will,—and Sosthenes the brother,—
text.KJV: 1:1 Paul called to be an Apostle of Iesus Christ, through the will of God, and Sosthenes our brother,
text.NABRE: 1:1 I. Address Chapter 1 - Greeting. Paul, called to be an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Sosthenes our brother,

text.NRSV: To the church of God that is in Corinth, to those who are sanctified in Christ Jesus, called to be saints, together with all those who in every place call on the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, both their Lord and ours:
text.Rotherham: Unto the assembly of God which is in Corinth, sanctified in Christ Jesus, called saints,—with all who call upon the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, in every place,—their Lord and ours:
text.KJV: 1:2 Unto the Church of God which is at Corinth, to them that are sanctified in Christ Iesus, called to be Saints, with all that in euery place call vpon the Name of Iesus Christ our Lord, both theirs and ours.
text.NABRE: 1:2 to the church of God that is in Corinth, to you who have been sanctified in Christ Jesus, called to be holy, with all those everywhere who call upon the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, their Lord and ours.

text.NRSV: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Favour unto you, and peace, from God our Father, and Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: 1:3 Grace be vnto you, and peace from God our Father, and from the Lord Iesus Christ.
text.NABRE: 1:3 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: I give thanks to my God always for you because of the grace of God that has been given you in Christ Jesus,
text.Rotherham: I give thanks unto my God, at all times, concerning you, by reason of the favour of God given unto you in Christ Jesus,—
text.KJV: 1:4 I thanke my God alwayes on your behalfe, for the grace of God which is giuen you by Iesus Christ,
text.NABRE: 1:4 Thanksgiving. I give thanks to my God always on your account for the grace of God bestowed on you in Christ Jesus,

text.NRSV: for in every way you have been enriched in him, in speech and knowledge of every kind—
text.Rotherham: That, in everything, ye have been enriched in him—in all discourse and in all knowledge;
text.KJV: 1:5 That in euery thing yee are enriched by him, in all vtterance, and in all knowledge:
text.NABRE: 1:5 that in him you were enriched in every way, with all discourse and all knowledge,

text.NRSV: just as the testimony of Christ has been strengthened among you—
text.Rotherham: Even as, the witness of the Christ, hath been confirmed in you,
text.KJV: 1:6 Euen as the Testimony of Christ was confirmed in you.
text.NABRE: 1:6 as the testimony to Christ was confirmed among you,

text.NRSV: so that you are not lacking in any spiritual gift as you wait for the revealing of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: So that ye come short in no gift of favour,—ardently awaiting the revelation of our Lord Jesus Christ:
text.KJV: 1:7 So that yee come behinde in no gift; wayting for the comming of our Lord Iesus Christ,
text.NABRE: 1:7 so that you are not lacking in any spiritual gift as you wait for the revelation of our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: He will also strengthen you to the end, so that you may be blameless on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Who will also confirm you unto the end, unaccusable in the day of our Lord Jesus [Christ]:
text.KJV: 1:8 Who shall also confirme you vnto the end, that yee may be blamelesse in the day of our Lord Iesus Christ.
text.NABRE: 1:8 He will keep you firm to the end, irreproachable on the day of our Lord Jesus [Christ].

text.NRSV: God is faithful; by him you were called into the fellowship of his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
text.Rotherham: Faithful, is God, through whom ye have been called into the fellowship of his Son Jesus Christ our Lord.
text.KJV: 1:9 God is faithful by whom ye were called vnto the felowship of his Sonne Iesus Christ our Lord.
text.NABRE: 1:9 God is faithful, and by him you were called to fellowship with his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.

text.NRSV: Now I appeal to you, brothers and sisters, by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that all of you be in agreement and that there be no divisions among you, but that you be united in the same mind and the same purpose.
text.Rotherham: But I beseech you, brethren, through the name of our Lord Jesus Christ,—that, the same thing, ye, all, be saying, and that there be not, among you, divisions; but that ye be fitly joined together—in the same mind, and in the same judgment.
text.KJV: 1:10 Now I beseech you brethren by the Name of our Lord Iesus Christ, that yee all speake the same thing, and that there be no diuisions among you: but that ye be perfectly ioyned together in the same minde, and in the same iudgement.
text.NABRE: 1:10 II. Disorders in the Corinthian Community A. Divisions in the Church - Groups and Slogans. I urge you, brothers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that all of you agree in what you say, and that there be no divisions among you, but that you be united in the same mind and in the same purpose.

text.NRSV: For it has been reported to me by Chloe’s people that there are quarrels among you, my brothers and sisters.
text.Rotherham: For it hath been signified unto me, concerning you, my brethren,—by them who are of [the household of] Chloe,—that there are strifes among you,
text.KJV: 1:11 For it hath bene declared vnto me of you, my brethren, by them which are of the house of Cloe, that there are contentions among you.
text.NABRE: 1:11 For it has been reported to me about you, my brothers, by Chloe’s people, that there are rivalries among you.

text.NRSV: What I mean is that each of you says, “I belong to Paul,” or “I belong to Apollos,” or “I belong to Cephas,” or “I belong to Christ.”
text.Rotherham: Now I mean this,—that, each one of you, is saying—I, indeed, am of Paul, but, I, of Apollos, but, I, of Cephas, but, I, of Christ:
text.KJV: 1:12 Now this I say, that euery one of you saith, I am of Paul, and I of Apollo, and I of Cephas, and I of Christ.
text.NABRE: 1:12 I mean that each of you is saying, “I belong to Paul,” or “I belong to Apollos,” or “I belong to Cephas,” or “I belong to Christ.”

text.NRSV: Has Christ been divided? Was Paul crucified for you? Or were you baptized in the name of Paul?
text.Rotherham: The Christ is divided! Was, Paul, crucified for you? Or, into the name of Paul, were ye immersed?
text.KJV: 1:13 Is Christ diuided? was Paul crucified for you? or were yee baptized in the name of Paul?
text.NABRE: 1:13 Is Christ divided? Was Paul crucified for you? Or were you baptized in the name of Paul?

text.NRSV: I thank God that I baptized none of you except Crispus and Gaius,
text.Rotherham: I give thanks, that, none of you, did I immerse—save Crispus and Gaius,
text.KJV: 1:14 I thanke God that I baptized none of you, but Crispus and Gaius:
text.NABRE: 1:14 I give thanks [to God] that I baptized none of you except Crispus and Gaius,

text.NRSV: so that no one can say that you were baptized in my name.
text.Rotherham: Lest any should say—into my own name, I immersed:
text.KJV: 1:15 Lest any should say, that I had baptized in mine owne name.
text.NABRE: 1:15 so that no one can say you were baptized in my name.

text.NRSV: (I did baptize also the household of Stephanas; beyond that, I do not know whether I baptized anyone else.)
text.Rotherham: Yea! I immersed the house of Stephanas also,—besides, I know not whether, anyone else, I immersed.
text.KJV: 1:16 And I baptized also the household of Stephanas: besides, I know not whether I baptized any other.
text.NABRE: 1:16 (I baptized the household of Stephanas also; beyond that I do not know whether I baptized anyone else.)

text.NRSV: For Christ did not send me to baptize but to proclaim the gospel, and not with eloquent wisdom, so that the cross of Christ might not be emptied of its power.
text.Rotherham: For Christ sent me not, to be immersing, but to be telling the good news,—not with wisdom of discourse, lest, void should be made, the cross of the Christ.
text.KJV: 1:17 For Christ sent me not to baptize, but to preach the Gospel: not with wisedome of words, lest the Crosse of Christ should be made of none effect.
text.NABRE: 1:17 For Christ did not send me to baptize but to preach the gospel, and not with the wisdom of human eloquence, so that the cross of Christ might not be emptied of its meaning.

text.NRSV: For the message about the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.
text.Rotherham: For, the discourse which concerneth the cross, unto them, indeed, who are perishing, is, foolishness; but, unto them who are being saved—unto us, it is, God’s power.
text.KJV: 1:18 For the preaching of the Crosse is to them that perish, foolishnesse: but vnto vs which are saued, it is the power of God.
text.NABRE: 1:18 Paradox of the Cross. The message of the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.

text.NRSV: For it is written, “I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and the discernment of the discerning I will thwart.”
text.Rotherham: For it is written—I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and, the discernment of the discerning, will I set aside.
text.KJV: 1:19 For it is written, I will destroy the wisedome of the wise, and wil bring to nothing the vnderstanding of the prudent.
text.NABRE: 1:19 For it is written: “I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and the learning of the learned I will set aside.”

text.NRSV: Where is the one who is wise? Where is the scribe? Where is the debater of this age? Has not God made foolish the wisdom of the world?
text.Rotherham: Where is the wise? Where is the scribe? Where is the disputer of this age? Hath not God made foolish the wisdom of the world?
text.KJV: 1:20 Where is the wise? where is the Scribe? where is the disputer of this world? Hath not God made foolish the wisedome of this world?
text.NABRE: 1:20 Where is the wise one? Where is the scribe? Where is the debater of this age? Has not God made the wisdom of the world foolish?

text.NRSV: For since, in the wisdom of God, the world did not know God through wisdom, God decided, through the foolishness of our proclamation, to save those who believe.
text.Rotherham: For, seeing that, in the wisdom of God, the world, through its wisdom, did not get to knew God, God was well-pleased—through the foolishness of the thing proclaimed, to save them that believe.
text.KJV: 1:21 For after that, in the wisedom of God, the world by wisedome knew not God, it pleased God by the foolishnesse of preaching, to saue them that beleeue.
text.NABRE: 1:21 For since in the wisdom of God the world did not come to know God through wisdom, it was the will of God through the foolishness of the proclamation to save those who have faith.

text.NRSV: For Jews demand signs and Greeks desire wisdom,
text.Rotherham: Seeing that both, Jews for signs, do ask, and, Greeks for wisdom, do seek,
text.KJV: 1:22 For the Iewes require a signe, and the Greekes seeke after wisedome.
text.NABRE: 1:22 For Jews demand signs and Greeks look for wisdom,

text.NRSV: but we proclaim Christ crucified, a stumbling block to Jews and foolishness to Gentiles,
text.Rotherham: Whereas, we, proclaim a Christ who hath been crucified,—unto Jews, indeed, an occasion of stumbling, and, unto Gentiles, foolishness;
text.KJV: 1:23 But wee preach Christ crucified, vnto the Iewes a stumbling block, and vnto the Greekes, foolishnesse:
text.NABRE: 1:23 but we proclaim Christ crucified, a stumbling block to Jews and foolishness to Gentiles,

text.NRSV: but to those who are the called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ the power of God and the wisdom of God.
text.Rotherham: But, unto the called themselves—both Jews and Greeks, Christ, God’s power, and, God’s wisdom.
text.KJV: 1:24 But vnto them which are called, both Iewes and Greekes, Christ, the power of God, & the wisedome of God.
text.NABRE: 1:24 but to those who are called, Jews and Greeks alike, Christ the power of God and the wisdom of God.

text.NRSV: For God’s foolishness is wiser than human wisdom, and God’s weakness is stronger than human strength.
text.Rotherham: Because, God’s foolish thing, is, wiser than men, and, God’s weak thing, mightier than men.
text.KJV: 1:25 Because the foolishnesse of God is wiser then men: and the weakenesse of God is stronger then men.
text.NABRE: 1:25 For the foolishness of God is wiser than human wisdom, and the weakness of God is stronger than human strength. The Corinthians and Paul.

text.NRSV: Consider your own call, brothers and sisters: not many of you were wise by human standards, not many were powerful, not many were of noble birth.
text.Rotherham: For be looking at the calling of you, brethren,—that [there were] not many wise, according to flesh. Not many powerful, not many high-born:
text.KJV: 1:26 For ye see your calling, brethren, how that not many wise men after the flesh, not many mighty, not many noble are called.
text.NABRE: 1:26 Consider your own calling, brothers. Not many of you were wise by human standards, not many were powerful, not many were of noble birth.

text.NRSV: But God chose what is foolish in the world to shame the wise; God chose what is weak in the world to shame the strong;
text.Rotherham: On the contrary—the foolish things of the world, hath God chosen, that he might put to shame them who are wise, and, the weak things of the world, hath God chosen, that he might put to shame the things that are mighty,
text.KJV: 1:27 But God hath chosen the foolish things of the world, to confound the wise: and God hath chosen the weake things of the world, to confound the things which are mighty:
text.NABRE: 1:27 Rather, God chose the foolish of the world to shame the wise, and God chose the weak of the world to shame the strong,

text.NRSV: God chose what is low and despised in the world, things that are not, to reduce to nothing things that are,
text.Rotherham: And, the low-born things of the world, and the things that are despised, hath God chosen,—[and] the things that are not,—that, the things that are, he might bring to nought;
text.KJV: 1:28 And base things of the world, and things which are despised, hath God chosen, yea and things which are not, to bring to nought things that are,
text.NABRE: 1:28 and God chose the lowly and despised of the world, those who count for nothing, to reduce to nothing those who are something,

text.NRSV: so that no one might boast in the presence of God.
text.Rotherham: So that no flesh should boast before God.
text.KJV: 1:29 That no flesh should glory in his presence.
text.NABRE: 1:29 so that no human being might boast before God.

text.NRSV: He is the source of your life in Christ Jesus, who became for us wisdom from God, and righteousness and sanctification and redemption,
text.Rotherham: But, of him, are, ye, in Christ Jesus, who hath been made wisdom unto us, from God,—both righteousness, and sanctification, and redemption:
text.KJV: 1:30 But of him are ye in Christ Iesus, who of God is made vnto vs wisedome, and righteousnesse, and sanctification, and redemption:
text.NABRE: 1:30 It is due to him that you are in Christ Jesus, who became for us wisdom from God, as well as righteousness, sanctification, and redemption,

text.NRSV: in order that, as it is written, “Let the one who boasts, boast in the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: In order that, even as it is written—He that boasteth, in the Lord, let him boast.
text.KJV: 1:31 That according as it is written, He that glorieth, let him glory in the Lord.
text.NABRE: 1:31 so that, as it is written, “Whoever boasts, should boast in the Lord.”

text.NRSV: When I came to you, brothers and sisters, I did not come proclaiming the mystery of God to you in lofty words or wisdom.
text.Rotherham: I, therefore, brethren, when I came unto you, came, not with excellency of discourse or wisdom, declaring unto you the mystery of God;
text.KJV: 2:1 And I, brethren, when I came to you, came not with excellencie of speech, or of wisedome, declaring vnto you the testimony of God.
text.NABRE: 2:1 Chapter 2 - When I came to you, brothers, proclaiming the mystery of God, I did not come with sublimity of words or of wisdom.

text.NRSV: For I decided to know nothing among you except Jesus Christ, and him crucified.
text.Rotherham: For I had not determined to know anything among you, save Jesus Christ,—and, him, as one who had been crucified!
text.KJV: 2:2 For I determined not to know any thing amōg you, saue Iesus Christ, and him crucified.
text.NABRE: 2:2 For I resolved to know nothing while I was with you except Jesus Christ, and him crucified.

text.NRSV: And I came to you in weakness and in fear and in much trembling.
text.Rotherham: I, therefore in weakness, and in fear, and in much trembling, came to be with you,
text.KJV: 2:3 And I was with you in weakenesse, and in feare, and in much trembling.
text.NABRE: 2:3 I came to you in weakness and fear and much trembling,

text.NRSV: My speech and my proclamation were not with plausible words of wisdom, but with a demonstration of the Spirit and of power,
text.Rotherham: And, my discourse, and what I proclaimed, were not in suasive words of wisdom, but in demonstration of Spirit and power:
text.KJV: 2:4 And my speech, and my preaching was not with entising words of mans wisedome, but in demonstration of the Spirit, and of power:
text.NABRE: 2:4 and my message and my proclamation were not with persuasive [words of] wisdom, but with a demonstration of spirit and power,

text.NRSV: so that your faith might rest not on human wisdom but on the power of God.
text.Rotherham: In order that, your faith, might not be in men’s wisdom, but in God’s power.
text.KJV: 2:5 That your faith should not stand in the wisdome of men, but in the power of God.
text.NABRE: 2:5 so that your faith might rest not on human wisdom but on the power of God. The True Wisdom.

text.NRSV: Yet among the mature we do speak wisdom, though it is not a wisdom of this age or of the rulers of this age, who are doomed to perish.
text.Rotherham: Wisdom, however, we do speak, among the full-grown,—wisdom, indeed, not of this age, nor of the rulers of this age, who are to come to nought;
text.KJV: 2:6 Howbeit wee speake wisedome among them that are perfect: yet not the wisedome of this worlde, nor of the Princes of this worlde, that come to nought:
text.NABRE: 2:6 Yet we do speak a wisdom to those who are mature, but not a wisdom of this age, nor of the rulers of this age who are passing away.

text.NRSV: But we speak God’s wisdom, secret and hidden, which God decreed before the ages for our glory.
text.Rotherham: But we speak, God’s, wisdom, in a sacred secret, that hidden [wisdom], which God marked out beforehand, before the ages, for our glory,—
text.KJV: 2:7 But wee speake the wisedome of God in a mysterie, euen the hidden wisedome which God ordeined before the world, vnto our glory.
text.NABRE: 2:7 Rather, we speak God’s wisdom, mysterious, hidden, which God predetermined before the ages for our glory,

text.NRSV: None of the rulers of this age understood this; for if they had, they would not have crucified the Lord of glory.
text.Rotherham: Which, none of the rulers of this age had come to know, for, had they known, not, in that case, the Lord of the glory, would they have crucified!
text.KJV: 2:8 Which none of the princes of this world knewe: for had they knowen it, they would not haue crucified the Lord of glory.
text.NABRE: 2:8 and which none of the rulers of this age knew; for, if they had known it, they would not have crucified the Lord of glory.

text.NRSV: But, as it is written, “What no eye has seen, nor ear heard, nor the human heart conceived, what God has prepared for those who love him”—
text.Rotherham: But, even as it is written—The things which eye hath not seen, and ear hath not heard, and upon the heart of man have not come up,—whatsoever things God hath prepared for them that love him,
text.KJV: 2:9 But as it is written, Eye hath not seene, nor eare heard, neither haue entred into the heart of man, the things which God hath prepared for them that loue him.
text.NABRE: 2:9 But as it is written: “What eye has not seen, and ear has not heard, and what has not entered the human heart, what God has prepared for those who love him,”

text.NRSV: these things God has revealed to us through the Spirit; for the Spirit searches everything, even the depths of God.
text.Rotherham: Unto us, in fact, hath God revealed through the Spirit; for, the Spirit, into all things, maketh search, yea! the deep things of God.
text.KJV: 2:10 But God hath reueiled them vnto vs by his Spirit: for the Spirit searcheth all things, yea, the deepe things of God.
text.NABRE: 2:10 this God has revealed to us through the Spirit. For the Spirit scrutinizes everything, even the depths of God.

text.NRSV: For what human being knows what is truly human except the human spirit that is within? So also no one comprehends what is truly God’s except the Spirit of God.
text.Rotherham: For who of men knoweth the things of a man,—save the spirit of the man that is in him? thus, even the things of God, hath no one come to know, save the Spirit of God.
text.KJV: 2:11 For what man knoweth the things of a man, saue the spirit of man which is in him? Euen so the things of God knoweth no man, but the Spirit of God.
text.NABRE: 2:11 Among human beings, who knows what pertains to a person except the spirit of the person that is within? Similarly, no one knows what pertains to God except the Spirit of God.

text.NRSV: Now we have received not the spirit of the world, but the Spirit that is from God, so that we may understand the gifts bestowed on us by God.
text.Rotherham: But, as for us,—not the spirit of the world, have we received, but the Spirit which is of God,—that we might know the things which, by God, have been given in favour unto us:—
text.KJV: 2:12 Now wee haue receiued, not the spirit of the world, but the Spirit which is of God, that wee might know the things that are freely giuen to vs of God.
text.NABRE: 2:12 We have not received the spirit of the world but the Spirit that is from God, so that we may understand the things freely given us by God.

text.NRSV: And we speak of these things in words not taught by human wisdom but taught by the Spirit, interpreting spiritual things to those who are spiritual.
text.Rotherham: Which we also speak—not in words taught of human wisdom, but in such as are taught of [the] Spirit, by spiritual words, spiritual things, explaining.
text.KJV: 2:13 Which things also we speake, not in the words which mans wisedome teacheth, but which the holy Ghost teacheth, comparing spiritual things with spirituall.
text.NABRE: 2:13 And we speak about them not with words taught by human wisdom, but with words taught by the Spirit, describing spiritual realities in spiritual terms.

text.NRSV: Those who are unspiritual do not receive the gifts of God’s Spirit, for they are foolishness to them, and they are unable to understand them because they are spiritually discerned.
text.Rotherham: But, a man of the soul, doth not welcome the things of the Spirit of God, for they are foolishness unto him, and he cannot get to know them, because, spiritually, are they examined;
text.KJV: 2:14 But the naturall man receiueth not the things of the Spirit of God, for they are foolishnesse vnto him: neither can he know them, because they are spiritually discerned.
text.NABRE: 2:14 Now the natural person does not accept what pertains to the Spirit of God, for to him it is foolishness, and he cannot understand it, because it is judged spiritually.

text.NRSV: Those who are spiritual discern all things, and they are themselves subject to no one else’s discernment.
text.Rotherham: But, the man of the spirit, on the one hand, examineth all things, but, on the other, he himself, by no one, is examined.
text.KJV: 2:15 But he that is spirituall, iudgeth all things, yet he himselfe is iudged of no man.
text.NABRE: 2:15 The spiritual person, however, can judge everything but is not subject to judgment by anyone.

text.NRSV: “For who has known the mind of the Lord so as to instruct him?” But we have the mind of Christ.
text.Rotherham: For who hath come to know the mind of the Lord, that shall instruct him? But, we, have, the mind of Christ.
text.KJV: 2:16 For who hath knowen the mind of the Lord that he may instruct him? But we haue the mind of Christ.
text.NABRE: 2:16 For “who has known the mind of the Lord, so as to counsel him?” But we have the mind of Christ.

text.NRSV: And so, brothers and sisters, I could not speak to you as spiritual people, but rather as people of the flesh, as infants in Christ.
text.Rotherham: I, therefore, brethren, have not been able to speak unto you, as unto men of the Spirit, but as unto men of the flesh—as unto babes in Christ:—
text.KJV: 3:1 And I, brethren, could not speake vnto you as vnto spirituall, but as vnto carnall, euen as vnto babes in Christ.
text.NABRE: 3:1 Chapter 3 - Brothers, I could not talk to you as spiritual people, but as fleshly people, as infants in Christ.

text.NRSV: I fed you with milk, not solid food, for you were not ready for solid food. Even now you are still not ready,
text.Rotherham: With milk, have I fed you, not, with meat; for, not yet, have ye been able;—nay! not [yet] even now, are ye able,
text.KJV: 3:2 I haue fed you with milke, and not with meate: for hitherto yee were not able to beare it, neither yet now are ye able.
text.NABRE: 3:2 I fed you milk, not solid food, because you were unable to take it. Indeed, you are still not able, even now,

text.NRSV: for you are still of the flesh. For as long as there is jealousy and quarreling among you, are you not of the flesh, and behaving according to human inclinations?
text.Rotherham: For ye are yet fleshly. For, whereas there are, among you, jealousy and strife, are ye not, fleshly, and, after the manner of men, walking?
text.KJV: 3:3 For ye are yet carnall: for whereas there is among you enuying, and strife, and diuisions, are ye not carnall, and walke as men?
text.NABRE: 3:3 for you are still of the flesh. While there is jealousy and rivalry among you, are you not of the flesh and behaving in an ordinary human way?

text.NRSV: For when one says, “I belong to Paul,” and another, “I belong to Apollos,” are you not merely human?
text.Rotherham: For, as soon as one beginneth to say—I, indeed, am of Paul! and another—I, of Apollos! are ye not, men?
text.KJV: 3:4 For while one saieth, I am of Paul, and another, I am of Apollo, are ye not carnall?
text.NABRE: 3:4 Whenever someone says, “I belong to Paul,” and another, “I belong to Apollos,” are you not merely human? The Role of God’s Ministers.

text.NRSV: What then is Apollos? What is Paul? Servants through whom you came to believe, as the Lord assigned to each.
text.Rotherham: What then is Apollos? and, what is Paul? ministers through whom ye believed, and, each, as the Lord, gave to him.
text.KJV: 3:5 Who then is Paul? and who is Apollo? but ministers by whom ye beleeued, euen as the Lord gaue to euery man.
text.NABRE: 3:5 What is Apollos, after all, and what is Paul? Ministers through whom you became believers, just as the Lord assigned each one.

text.NRSV: I planted, Apollos watered, but God gave the growth.
text.Rotherham: I, planted, Apollos, watered,—but, God, caused to, grow.
text.KJV: 3:6 I haue planted, Apollo watered: but God gaue the encrease.
text.NABRE: 3:6 I planted, Apollos watered, but God caused the growth.

text.NRSV: So neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything, but only God who gives the growth.
text.Rotherham: So that, [neither] is he that planteth anything, nor, he that watereth,—but, God, who causeth to, grow.
text.KJV: 3:7 So then, neither is he that planteth any thing, neither hee that watereth: but God that giueth the increase.
text.NABRE: 3:7 Therefore, neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything, but only God, who causes the growth.

text.NRSV: The one who plants and the one who waters have a common purpose, and each will receive wages according to the labor of each.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, he that planteth and he that watereth, are one:—howbeit, each one, his own reward, shall receive,—according to his own labour.
text.KJV: 3:8 Now hee that planteth, and hee that watereth, are one: and euery man shal receiue his own reward according to his owne labour.
text.NABRE: 3:8 The one who plants and the one who waters are equal, and each will receive wages in proportion to his labor.

text.NRSV: For we are God’s servants, working together; you are God’s field, God’s building.
text.Rotherham: For we are, God’s, fellow-workmen: ye are, God’s, farm, God’s building.
text.KJV: 3:9 For wee are labourers together with God, ye are Gods husbandry, yee are Gods building.
text.NABRE: 3:9 For we are God’s co-workers; you are God’s field, God’s building.

text.NRSV: According to the grace of God given to me, like a skilled master builder I laid a foundation, and someone else is building on it. Each builder must choose with care how to build on it.
text.Rotherham: According to the favour of God which hath been given unto me, as a wise master-builder, I laid a foundation, whereas, another, is building thereon; but, let each one, see, how he buildeth thereon;
text.KJV: 3:10 According to the grace of God which is giuen vnto mee, as a wise master builder I haue laid the foundation, and another buildeth thereon. But let euery man take heede how hee buildeth thereupon.
text.NABRE: 3:10 According to the grace of God given to me, like a wise master builder I laid a foundation, and another is building upon it. But each one must be careful how he builds upon it,

text.NRSV: For no one can lay any foundation other than the one that has been laid; that foundation is Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, other foundation, can, no one, lay, than that which is lying, which is, Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: 3:11 For other foundation can no man lay, then that is laide, which is Iesus Christ.
text.NABRE: 3:11 for no one can lay a foundation other than the one that is there, namely, Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: Now if anyone builds on the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, hay, straw—
text.Rotherham: But, if anyone is building, upon the foundation—gold, silver, precious stones, wood, hay, straw,
text.KJV: 3:12 Now if any man build vpon this foundation, gold, siluer, preciousstones, wood, hay, stubble:
text.NABRE: 3:12 If anyone builds on this foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, hay, or straw,

text.NRSV: the work of each builder will become visible, for the Day will disclose it, because it will be revealed with fire, and the fire will test what sort of work each has done.
text.Rotherham: Each one’s work, shall be made, manifest; for, the day, will make it plain, because, by fire, is it to be revealed,—and, each one’s work, of what sort it is, the fire itself will prove:—
text.KJV: 3:13 Euery mans worke shall be made manifest. For the day shall declare it, because it shall bee reuealed by fire, and the fire shall trie euery mans worke of what sort it is.
text.NABRE: 3:13 the work of each will come to light, for the Day will disclose it. It will be revealed with fire, and the fire [itself] will test the quality of each one’s work.

text.NRSV: If what has been built on the foundation survives, the builder will receive a reward.
text.Rotherham: If, anyone’s work, shall abide, which he built, a reward, shall he receive,—
text.KJV: 3:14 If any mans worke abide which he hath built thereupon, he shal receiue a reward.
text.NABRE: 3:14 If the work stands that someone built upon the foundation, that person will receive a wage.

text.NRSV: If the work is burned up, the builder will suffer loss; the builder will be saved, but only as through fire.
text.Rotherham: If, anyone’s work, shall be burnt up, he shall suffer loss, but shall, himself, be saved—though, thus, as through fire.
text.KJV: 3:15 If any mans worke shall bee burnt, he shall suffer losse: but he himselfe shall be saued: yet so, as by fire.
text.NABRE: 3:15 But if someone’s work is burned up, that one will suffer loss; the person will be saved, but only as through fire.

text.NRSV: Do you not know that you are God’s temple and that God’s Spirit dwells in you?
text.Rotherham: Know ye not that ye are a shrine of God, and that the Spirit of God within you doth dwell?
text.KJV: 3:16 Knowe yee not that yee are the Temple of God, and that the Spirit of God dwelleth in you?
text.NABRE: 3:16 Do you not know that you are the temple of God, and that the Spirit of God dwells in you?

text.NRSV: If anyone destroys God’s temple, God will destroy that person. For God’s temple is holy, and you are that temple.
text.Rotherham: If anyone doth mar the shrine of God, God, will mar him; for the shrine of God is holy,—and such are ye.
text.KJV: 3:17 If any man defile the Temple of God, him shall God destroy: for the Temple of God is holy, which Temple ye are.
text.NABRE: 3:17 If anyone destroys God’s temple, God will destroy that person; for the temple of God, which you are, is holy.

text.NRSV: Do not deceive yourselves. If you think that you are wise in this age, you should become fools so that you may become wise.
text.Rotherham: Let no one be deceiving himself:—if anyone imagineth himself to be wise among you, in this age, let him become foolish, that he may become wise;
text.KJV: 3:18 Let no man deceiue himselfe: If any man among you seemeth to bee wise in this world, let him become a foole, that he may be wise.
text.NABRE: 3:18 Let no one deceive himself. If anyone among you considers himself wise in this age, let him become a fool so as to become wise.

text.NRSV: For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God. For it is written, “He catches the wise in their craftiness,”
text.Rotherham: For, the wisdom of this world, is, foolishness with God; for it is written—He that taketh the wise in their knavery,—
text.KJV: 3:19 For the wisedome of this world is foolishnesse with God: for it is written, Hee taketh the wise in their owne craftinesse.
text.NABRE: 3:19 For the wisdom of this world is foolishness in the eyes of God, for it is written: “He catches the wise in their own ruses,”

text.NRSV: and again, “The Lord knows the thoughts of the wise, that they are futile.”
text.Rotherham: And again—The Lord taketh note of the speculations of the wise, that they are vain.
text.KJV: 3:20 And againe, The Lord knoweth the thoughts of the wise, that they are vaine.
text.NABRE: 3:20 and again: “The Lord knows the thoughts of the wise, that they are vain.”

text.NRSV: So let no one boast about human leaders. For all things are yours,
text.Rotherham: So then, let, no one, be boasting in men; for, all things, are yours—
text.KJV: 3:21 Therefore let no man glory in men, for all things are yours.
text.NABRE: 3:21 So let no one boast about human beings, for everything belongs to you,

text.NRSV: whether Paul or Apollos or Cephas or the world or life or death or the present or the future—all belong to you,
text.Rotherham: Whether Paul, or Apollos, or Cephas, or the world, or life, or death, or things present, or things to come, all, are, yours,
text.KJV: 3:22 Whether Paul, or Apollo, or Cephas, or the world, or life, or death, or things present, or things to come, all are yours.
text.NABRE: 3:22 Paul or Apollos or Cephas, or the world or life or death, or the present or the future: all belong to you,

text.NRSV: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
text.Rotherham: And, ye, are Christ’s,—and, Christ, is, God’s.
text.KJV: 3:23 And yee are Christs, and Christ is Gods.
text.NABRE: 3:23 and you to Christ, and Christ to God.

text.NRSV: Think of us in this way, as servants of Christ and stewards of God’s mysteries.
text.Rotherham: Let a man, so, be reckoning of us, as officers of Christ, and stewards of sacred secrets of God.
text.KJV: 4:1 Let a man so account of vs, as of the ministers of Christ, and stewards of the mysteries of God.
text.NABRE: 4:1 Chapter 4 - Thus should one regard us: as servants of Christ and stewards of the mysteries of God.

text.NRSV: Moreover, it is required of stewards that they be found trustworthy.
text.Rotherham: Here, furthermore, it is sought in stewards, that, faithful, one be found.
text.KJV: 4:2 Moreouer, it is required in stewards, that a man be found faithfull.
text.NABRE: 4:2 Now it is of course required of stewards that they be found trustworthy.

text.NRSV: But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged by you or by any human court. I do not even judge myself.
text.Rotherham: With me, however, it counteth for the very smallest thing, that, by you, I should be examined, or by a human day. Nay! I am not even examining myself,
text.KJV: 4:3 But with mee it is a very small thing that I should bee iudged of you, or of mans iudgement: yea, I iudge not mine owne selfe.
text.NABRE: 4:3 It does not concern me in the least that I be judged by you or any human tribunal; I do not even pass judgment on myself;

text.NRSV: I am not aware of anything against myself, but I am not thereby acquitted. It is the Lord who judges me.
text.Rotherham: For, of nothing, to myself, am I conscious: nevertheless, not hereby, am I declared righteous, but, he that doth examine me, is the Lord.
text.KJV: 4:4 For I know nothing by my selfe, yet am I not hereby iustified: but hee that iudgeth me is the Lord.
text.NABRE: 4:4 I am not conscious of anything against me, but I do not thereby stand acquitted; the one who judges me is the Lord.

text.NRSV: Therefore do not pronounce judgment before the time, before the Lord comes, who will bring to light the things now hidden in darkness and will disclose the purposes of the heart. Then each one will receive commendation from God.
text.Rotherham: So then, not before the fitting time, be judging anything,—until the Lord shall come, who will both bring to light the hidden things of darkness, and make manifest the counsels of the hearts; and, then, the praise shall come to each one, from God.
text.KJV: 4:5 Therefore iudge nothing before the time, vntill the Lord come, who both will bring to light the hidden things of darkenesse, and will make manifest the counsels of the hearts: and then shall euery man haue prayse of God.
text.NABRE: 4:5 Therefore, do not make any judgment before the appointed time, until the Lord comes, for he will bring to light what is hidden in darkness and will manifest the motives of our hearts, and then everyone will receive praise from God. Paul’s Life as Pattern.

text.NRSV: I have applied all this to myself and Apollos for your benefit, brothers and sisters, so that you may learn through us the meaning of the saying, “Nothing beyond what is written,” so that none of you will be puffed up in favor of one against another.
text.Rotherham: But, these things, brethren, have I transferred unto myself and Apollos, for your sakes, that, in us, ye might learn [the lesson] —Not beyond the things that are written…! that ye do not puff yourselves up, individually, for this one against that.
text.KJV: 4:6 And these things, brethren, I haue in a figure transferred to my selfe, and to Apollo, for your sakes: that ye might learne in vs not to thinke of men, aboue that which is written, that no one of you bee puffed vp for one against another.
text.NABRE: 4:6 I have applied these things to myself and Apollos for your benefit, brothers, so that you may learn from us not to go beyond what is written, so that none of you will be inflated with pride in favor of one person over against another.

text.NRSV: For who concedes you any superiority? What do you have that you did not receive? And if you received it, why do you boast as if it were not a gift?
text.Rotherham: For who maketh thee to differ? and what hast thou which thou didst not receive? But, if thou didst even receive it, why dost thou boast, as though thou hadst not received it?
text.KJV: 4:7 For who maketh thee to differ from another? And what hast thou that thou didst not receiue? Now if thou didst receiue it, why doest thou glory as if thou hadst not receiued it?
text.NABRE: 4:7 Who confers distinction upon you? What do you possess that you have not received? But if you have received it, why are you boasting as if you did not receive it?

text.NRSV: Already you have all you want! Already you have become rich! Quite apart from us you have become kings! Indeed, I wish that you had become kings, so that we might be kings with you!
text.Rotherham: Already, ye have become full, already, ye are become rich,—apart from us, ye are become kings! And I would indeed ye had become kings, that, we also, with you, might have together become kings!
text.KJV: 4:8 Now ye are full, now ye are rich, ye haue reigned as kings without vs, and I would to God ye did reigne, that we also might reigne with you.
text.NABRE: 4:8 You are already satisfied; you have already grown rich; you have become kings without us! Indeed, I wish that you had become kings, so that we also might become kings with you.

text.NRSV: For I think that God has exhibited us apostles as last of all, as though sentenced to death, because we have become a spectacle to the world, to angels and to mortals.
text.Rotherham: For I think that, God, hath set forth, us the apostles, to be last of all, as men devoted to death,—in that, a spectacle, have we been made, unto the world,—both unto messengers and unto men.
text.KJV: 4:9 For I thinke that God hath set forth vs the Apostles last, as it were approued to death. For wee are made a spectacle vnto the world, and to Angels, and to men.
text.NABRE: 4:9 For as I see it, God has exhibited us apostles as the last of all, like people sentenced to death, since we have become a spectacle to the world, to angels and human beings alike.

text.NRSV: We are fools for the sake of Christ, but you are wise in Christ. We are weak, but you are strong. You are held in honor, but we in disrepute.
text.Rotherham: We, are foolish for Christ’s sake, but, ye, prudent in Christ; we are weak, but, ye, mighty; ye, all-glorious, but, we, dishonoured.
text.KJV: 4:10 We are fooles for Christs sake, but ye are wise in Christ. We are weake, but ye are strong: yee are honourable, but we are despised.
text.NABRE: 4:10 We are fools on Christ’s account, but you are wise in Christ; we are weak, but you are strong; you are held in honor, but we in disrepute.

text.NRSV: To the present hour we are hungry and thirsty, we are poorly clothed and beaten and homeless,
text.Rotherham: Until the present hour, we both hunger and thirst, and are naked, and are buffeted, and are wanderers,
text.KJV: 4:11 Euen vnto this present houre we both hunger and thirst, and are naked, and are buffeted, and haue no certaine dwelling place,
text.NABRE: 4:11 To this very hour we go hungry and thirsty, we are poorly clad and roughly treated, we wander about homeless

text.NRSV: and we grow weary from the work of our own hands. When reviled, we bless; when persecuted, we endure;
text.Rotherham: And toil, working with our own hands: being reviled, we bless, being persecuted, we hold on,
text.KJV: 4:12 And labour, working with our owne hands: being reuiled, wee blesse: being persecuted, we suffer it:
text.NABRE: 4:12 and we toil, working with our own hands. When ridiculed, we bless; when persecuted, we endure;

text.NRSV: when slandered, we speak kindly. We have become like the rubbish of the world, the dregs of all things, to this very day.
text.Rotherham: Being defamed, we beseech: as the sweepings of the world have we become, the offscouring of all—until even now.
text.KJV: 4:13 Being defamed, we intreate: we are made as the filth of the world, and are the off-scouring of all things vnto this day.
text.NABRE: 4:13 when slandered, we respond gently. We have become like the world’s rubbish, the scum of all, to this very moment.

text.NRSV: I am not writing this to make you ashamed, but to admonish you as my beloved children.
text.Rotherham: Not, to shame you, write I these things; but, as my beloved children, I admonish you.
text.KJV: 4:14 I write not these things to shame you, but as my beloued sonnes I warne you.
text.NABRE: 4:14 I am writing you this not to shame you, but to admonish you as my beloved children.

text.NRSV: For though you might have ten thousand guardians in Christ, you do not have many fathers. Indeed, in Christ Jesus I became your father through the gospel.
text.Rotherham: For, though myriads of tutors ye should have in Christ, yet not many fathers; for, in Christ Jesus, through means of the joyful message, I, begat you.
text.KJV: 4:15 For though you haue ten thousand instructors in Christ, yet haue yee not many fathers: For in Christ Iesus I haue begotten you through the Gospel.
text.NABRE: 4:15 Even if you should have countless guides to Christ, yet you do not have many fathers, for I became your father in Christ Jesus through the gospel.

text.NRSV: I appeal to you, then, be imitators of me.
text.Rotherham: I beseech you, therefore,—become imitators of me.
text.KJV: 4:16 Wherefore I beseech you, be yee followers of me.
text.NABRE: 4:16 Therefore, I urge you, be imitators of me.

text.NRSV: For this reason I sent you Timothy, who is my beloved and faithful child in the Lord, to remind you of my ways in Christ Jesus, as I teach them everywhere in every church.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, sent I unto you, Timothy,—who is my beloved and faithful child in the Lord, who shall put, you, in mind of my ways which are in Christ [Jesus], even as, everywhere, in every assembly, I teach.
text.KJV: 4:17 For this cause haue I sent vnto you Timotheus, who is my beloued sonne, and faithfull in the Lord, who shal bring you into remembrance of my wayes which be in Christ, as I teach euery where in euery Church.
text.NABRE: 4:17 For this reason I am sending you Timothy, who is my beloved and faithful son in the Lord; he will remind you of my ways in Christ [Jesus], just as I teach them everywhere in every church.

text.NRSV: But some of you, thinking that I am not coming to you, have become arrogant.
text.Rotherham: But, as though I were not coming unto you, some are puffed up!
text.KJV: 4:18 Nowe some are puffed vp as though I would not come to you.
text.NABRE: 4:18 Some have become inflated with pride, as if I were not coming to you.

text.NRSV: But I will come to you soon, if the Lord wills, and I will find out not the talk of these arrogant people but their power.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, I will come quickly unto you,—if, the Lord, please,—and will get to know—not the speech of them who are puffed up, but, the power;
text.KJV: 4:19 But I wil come to you shortly, if the Lord will, and will knowe, not the speach of them which are puffed vp, but the power.
text.NABRE: 4:19 But I will come to you soon, if the Lord is willing, and I shall ascertain not the talk of these inflated people but their power.

text.NRSV: For the kingdom of God depends not on talk but on power.
text.Rotherham: For, not in speech, is the kingdom of God, but, in power.
text.KJV: 4:20 For the kingdome of God is not in word, but in power.
text.NABRE: 4:20 For the kingdom of God is not a matter of talk but of power.

text.NRSV: What would you prefer? Am I to come to you with a stick, or with love in a spirit of gentleness?
text.Rotherham: What will ye? that, with a rod, I should come unto you? or, with love, and a spirit of meekness?
text.KJV: 4:21 What will ye? Shall I come vnto you with a rod, or in loue, and in the spirit of meekenesse?
text.NABRE: 4:21 Which do you prefer? Shall I come to you with a rod, or with love and a gentle spirit?

text.NRSV: It is actually reported that there is sexual immorality among you, and of a kind that is not found even among Gentiles; for a man is living with his father’s wife.
text.Rotherham: On all hands, there is reported to be among you—fornication, and such fornication as this—which, not among the nations, [is found],—as that one should have, his father’s wife:—
text.KJV: 5:1 It is reported commonly, that there is fornication among you, and such fornication, as is not so much as named amongst the Gentiles, that one should haue his fathers wife.
text.NABRE: 5:1 B. Moral Disorders Chapter 5 - A Case of Incest. It is widely reported that there is immorality among you, and immorality of a kind not found even among pagans—a man living with his father’s wife.

text.NRSV: And you are arrogant! Should you not rather have mourned, so that he who has done this would have been removed from among you?
text.Rotherham: And, ye, have become puffed up, and have not rather mourned, in order that he might be removed out of your midst, who, this deed, hath wrought.
text.KJV: 5:2 And yee are puffed vp, and haue not rather mourned, that he that hath done this deed, might bee taken away from among you.
text.NABRE: 5:2 And you are inflated with pride. Should you not rather have been sorrowful? The one who did this deed should be expelled from your midst.

text.NRSV: For though absent in body, I am present in spirit; and as if present I have already pronounced judgment
text.Rotherham: For, I, indeed,—being absent in the body, but present in the spirit, have already judged, as present, him who, thus, this thing hath perpetrated:—
text.KJV: 5:3 For I verily as absent in body, but present in spirit, haue iudged alreadie, as though I were present, concerning him that hath so done this deed,
text.NABRE: 5:3 I, for my part, although absent in body but present in spirit, have already, as if present, pronounced judgment on the one who has committed this deed,

text.NRSV: in the name of the Lord Jesus on the man who has done such a thing. When you are assembled, and my spirit is present with the power of our Lord Jesus,
text.Rotherham: In the name of our Lord Jesus, ye being gathered together, and my spirit, with the power of our Lord Jesus,
text.KJV: 5:4 In the Name of our Lord Iesus Christ, when yee are gathered together, and my spirit, with the power of our Lord Iesus Christ,
text.NABRE: 5:4 in the name of [our] Lord Jesus: when you have gathered together and I am with you in spirit with the power of the Lord Jesus,

text.NRSV: you are to hand this man over to Satan for the destruction of the flesh, so that his spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: To deliver such a one as this, unto Satan, for the destruction of the flesh,—that, the spirit, may be saved in the day of the Lord.
text.KJV: 5:5 To deliuer such a one vnto Satan for the destruction of the flesh, that the spirit may be saued in the day of the Lord Iesus.
text.NABRE: 5:5 you are to deliver this man to Satan for the destruction of his flesh, so that his spirit may be saved on the day of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Your boasting is not a good thing. Do you not know that a little yeast leavens the whole batch of dough?
text.Rotherham: Unseemly, is your boast! Know ye not that, a little leaven, doth leaven, the whole of the lump?
text.KJV: 5:6 Your glorying is not good: know ye not that a little leauen leaueneth the whole lumpe?
text.NABRE: 5:6 Your boasting is not appropriate. Do you not know that a little yeast leavens all the dough?

text.NRSV: Clean out the old yeast so that you may be a new batch, as you really are unleavened. For our paschal lamb, Christ, has been sacrificed.
text.Rotherham: Purge ye out the old leaven, that ye may be a new lump,—even as ye are unleavened; for, our passover, hath even been sacrificed—Christ:
text.KJV: 5:7 Purge out therefore the olde leauen, that ye may be a new lumpe, as ye are vnleauened. For euen Christ our Passeouer is sacrificed for vs.
text.NABRE: 5:7 Clear out the old yeast, so that you may become a fresh batch of dough, inasmuch as you are unleavened. For our paschal lamb, Christ, has been sacrificed.

text.NRSV: Therefore, let us celebrate the festival, not with the old yeast, the yeast of malice and evil, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.
text.Rotherham: So then, let us be keeping the feast, not with old leaven, nor with leaven of baseness and wickedness,—but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.
text.KJV: 5:8 Therefore let vs keepe the Feast, not with old leauen, neither with the leauen of malice and wickednesse: but with the vnleauened bread of sinceritie and trueth.
text.NABRE: 5:8 Therefore, let us celebrate the feast, not with the old yeast, the yeast of malice and wickedness, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.

text.NRSV: I wrote to you in my letter not to associate with sexually immoral persons—
text.Rotherham: I wrote unto you in my letter—not to be mixing yourselves up with fornicators;—
text.KJV: 5:9 I wrote vnto you in an Epistle, not to company with fornicators.
text.NABRE: 5:9 I wrote you in my letter not to associate with immoral people,

text.NRSV: not at all meaning the immoral of this world, or the greedy and robbers, or idolaters, since you would then need to go out of the world.
text.Rotherham: Not at all, [meaning] the fornicators of this world, or the covetous and extortioners, or idolaters,—else had ye been obliged, in that case, to go out of the world!
text.KJV: 5:10 Yet not altogether with the fornicatours of this world, or with the couetous, or extortioners, or with idolaters; for then must yee needs goe out of the world.
text.NABRE: 5:10 not at all referring to the immoral of this world or the greedy and robbers or idolaters; for you would then have to leave the world.

text.NRSV: But now I am writing to you not to associate with anyone who bears the name of brother or sister who is sexually immoral or greedy, or is an idolater, reviler, drunkard, or robber. Do not even eat with such a one.
text.Rotherham: But, now, I have written unto you not to be mixing yourselves up,—if anyone named a brother, be a fornicator, or covetous, or an idolater, or a reviler, or a drunkard, or an extortioner, with such a one as this, not so much, as to be eating together,
text.KJV: 5:11 But now I haue written vnto you, not to keepe company, if any man that is called a brother bee a fornicator, or couetous, or an idolater, or a railer, or a drunkard, or an extortioner: with such a one, no, not to eate.
text.NABRE: 5:11 But I now write to you not to associate with anyone named a brother, if he is immoral, greedy, an idolater, a slanderer, a drunkard, or a robber, not even to eat with such a person.

text.NRSV: For what have I to do with judging outsiders? Is it not those who are inside whom you are to judge?
text.Rotherham: For what have I to do to be judging them who are without? Do, ye, not judge, them who are within,
text.KJV: 5:12 For what haue I to doe to iudge them also that are without? doe not ye iudge them that are within?
text.NABRE: 5:12 For why should I be judging outsiders? Is it not your business to judge those within?

text.NRSV: God will judge those outside. “Drive out the wicked person from among yourselves.”
text.Rotherham: Whereas, them who are without, God, judgeth? Remove ye the wicked man from among, yourselves.
text.KJV: 5:13 But them that are without, God iudgeth. Therefore put away from among your selues that wicked person.
text.NABRE: 5:13 God will judge those outside. “Purge the evil person from your midst.”

text.NRSV: When any of you has a grievance against another, do you dare to take it to court before the unrighteous, instead of taking it before the saints?
text.Rotherham: Dare any of you, having, a matter against his brother, sue for judgment before the unrighteous, and not before the saints?
text.KJV: 6:1 Dare any of you, hauing a matter against another, goe to law before the vniust, and not before the Saints?
text.NABRE: 6:1 Chapter 6 - Lawsuits Before Unbelievers. How can any one of you with a case against another dare to bring it to the unjust for judgment instead of to the holy ones?

text.NRSV: Do you not know that the saints will judge the world? And if the world is to be judged by you, are you incompetent to try these smallest cases?
text.Rotherham: Or know ye not that, the saints, shall judge the world? And, if, by you, the world, is to be judged, unworthy are ye of the smallest judgment-seats?
text.KJV: 6:2 Do ye not know that the Saints shall iudge the world? And if the world shalbe iudged by you, are ye vnworthy to iudge the smallest matters?
text.NABRE: 6:2 Do you not know that the holy ones will judge the world? If the world is to be judged by you, are you unqualified for the lowest law courts?

text.NRSV: Do you not know that we are to judge angels—to say nothing of ordinary matters?
text.Rotherham: Know ye not that we shall judge, messengers? and not, then, matters of this life?
text.KJV: 6:3 Know ye not that we shall iudge Angels? How much more things that perteine to this life?
text.NABRE: 6:3 Do you not know that we will judge angels? Then why not everyday matters?

text.NRSV: If you have ordinary cases, then, do you appoint as judges those who have no standing in the church?
text.Rotherham: If, then, for matters of this, life ye have judgment-seats, them who are of no account in the assembly, these, are ye seating thereupon?
text.KJV: 6:4 If then yee haue iudgements of things perteining to this life, set them to iudge who are least esteemed in the Church.
text.NABRE: 6:4 If, therefore, you have courts for everyday matters, do you seat as judges people of no standing in the church?

text.NRSV: I say this to your shame. Can it be that there is no one among you wise enough to decide between one believer and another,
text.Rotherham: With a view to shame you, am I speaking. So, is it possible that there is among you—not so much as one wise man, who shall be able to judge between his brethren,—
text.KJV: 6:5 I speake to your shame. Is it so, that there is not a wise man amongst you? no not one that shall bee able to iudge betweene his brethren?
text.NABRE: 6:5 I say this to shame you. Can it be that there is not one among you wise enough to be able to settle a case between brothers?

text.NRSV: but a believer goes to court against a believer—and before unbelievers at that?
text.Rotherham: But, brother with brother, sueth for judgment, and that before unbelievers?
text.KJV: 6:6 But brother goeth to law with brother, & that before the vnbeleeuers?
text.NABRE: 6:6 But rather brother goes to court against brother, and that before unbelievers?

text.NRSV: In fact, to have lawsuits at all with one another is already a defeat for you. Why not rather be wronged? Why not rather be defrauded?
text.Rotherham: Already, indeed, it is an utter defeat for you, that ye are having, law-suits, one with another. Wherefore are ye not rather taking wrong? Wherefore are ye not rather suffering yourselves to be defrauded?
text.KJV: 6:7 Now therefore, there is vtterly a fault among you, because yee goe to law one with another: Why doe ye not rather take wrong? Why doe yee not rather suffer your selues to be defrauded?
text.NABRE: 6:7 Now indeed [then] it is, in any case, a failure on your part that you have lawsuits against one another. Why not rather put up with injustice? Why not rather let yourselves be cheated?

text.NRSV: But you yourselves wrong and defraud—and believers at that.
text.Rotherham: Nay! but, ye, are doing wrong, and defrauding,—and that [your] brethren.
text.KJV: 6:8 Nay, you do wrong and defraud, and that your brethren.
text.NABRE: 6:8 Instead, you inflict injustice and cheat, and this to brothers.

text.NRSV: Do you not know that wrongdoers will not inherit the kingdom of God? Do not be deceived! Fornicators, idolaters, adulterers, male prostitutes, sodomites,
text.Rotherham: Or know ye not that, wrong-doers, shall not inherit, God’s kingdom? Be not deceiving yourselves:—neither fornicators, nor idolaters, nor adulterers, nor effeminate, nor sodomites,
text.KJV: 6:9 Know yee not that the vnrighteous shall not inherite the kingdome of God? Be not deceiued: neither fornicatours, nor idolaters, nor adulterers, nor effeminate, nor abusers of themselues with mankinde,
text.NABRE: 6:9 Do you not know that the unjust will not inherit the kingdom of God? Do not be deceived; neither fornicators nor idolaters nor adulterers nor boy prostitutes nor sodomites

text.NRSV: thieves, the greedy, drunkards, revilers, robbers—none of these will inherit the kingdom of God.
text.Rotherham: Nor thieves, nor covetous, nor drunkards, nor revilers, nor extortioners—shall inherit, God’s kingdom.
text.KJV: 6:10 Nor theeues, nor couetous, nor drunkards, nor reuilers, nor extortioners, shall inherit the kingdom of God.
text.NABRE: 6:10 nor thieves nor the greedy nor drunkards nor slanderers nor robbers will inherit the kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: And this is what some of you used to be. But you were washed, you were sanctified, you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in the Spirit of our God.
text.Rotherham: And, these things, were some of you; but ye bathed them away,—but ye were sanctified, but ye were justified, in the name of [our] Lord Jesus Christ, and in the Spirit of our God.
text.KJV: 6:11 And such were some of you: but ye are washed, but ye are sanctified, but ye are iustified in the Name of the Lord Iesus, and by the Spirit of our God.
text.NABRE: 6:11 That is what some of you used to be; but now you have had yourselves washed, you were sanctified, you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in the Spirit of our God. Sexual Immorality.

text.NRSV: “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things are beneficial. “All things are lawful for me,” but I will not be dominated by anything.
text.Rotherham: All things, unto me, are allowable, but, not all things, are profitable: all things, unto me, are allowable, but, I, will not be brought under authority by any.
text.KJV: 6:12 All things are lawfull vnto mee, but all things are not expedient: all things are lawfull for mee, but I will not bee brought vnder the power of any.
text.NABRE: 6:12 “Everything is lawful for me,” but not everything is beneficial. “Everything is lawful for me,” but I will not let myself be dominated by anything.

text.NRSV: “Food is meant for the stomach and the stomach for food,” and God will destroy both one and the other. The body is meant not for fornication but for the Lord, and the Lord for the body.
text.Rotherham: Foods for the belly, and the belly for foods; but, God, will set aside, both it and them. The body, however, is not for fornication, but, for the Lord,—and, the Lord, for the body.
text.KJV: 6:13 Meats for the belly, and the belly for meates: but God shall destroy both it and them. Now the body is not for fornication, but for the Lord: and the Lord for the body.
text.NABRE: 6:13 “Food for the stomach and the stomach for food,” but God will do away with both the one and the other. The body, however, is not for immorality, but for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body;

text.NRSV: And God raised the Lord and will also raise us by his power.
text.Rotherham: God, moreover, hath both raised up, the Lord, and will raise, us, up from among [the dead] through his power.
text.KJV: 6:14 And God hath both raised vp the Lord, and will also raise vp vs by his owne power.
text.NABRE: 6:14 God raised the Lord and will also raise us by his power.

text.NRSV: Do you not know that your bodies are members of Christ? Should I therefore take the members of Christ and make them members of a prostitute? Never!
text.Rotherham: Know ye not that, your bodies, are, members of Christ? Shall I, then, take away the members of the Christ and make them members of a harlot? Far be it!
text.KJV: 6:15 Know yee not that your bodies are the members of Christ? Shall I then take the members of Christ, and make them the members of an harlot? God forbid.
text.NABRE: 6:15 Do you not know that your bodies are members of Christ? Shall I then take Christ’s members and make them the members of a prostitute? Of course not!

text.NRSV: Do you not know that whoever is united to a prostitute becomes one body with her? For it is said, “The two shall be one flesh.”
text.Rotherham: Or know ye not that, he that joineth himself unto the harlot, is, one body? For, saith he, the two, shall become, one flesh;
text.KJV: 6:16 What, know ye not that he which is ioyned to an harlot, is one body? for two (saith he) shalbe one flesh.
text.NABRE: 6:16 [Or] do you not know that anyone who joins himself to a prostitute becomes one body with her? For “the two,” it says, “will become one flesh.”

text.NRSV: But anyone united to the Lord becomes one spirit with him.
text.Rotherham: But, he that joineth himself unto the Lord, is, one Spirit.
text.KJV: 6:17 But hee that is ioyned vnto the Lord, is one spirit.
text.NABRE: 6:17 But whoever is joined to the Lord becomes one spirit with him.

text.NRSV: Shun fornication! Every other sin a person commits is outside the body; but the fornicator sins against the body itself.
text.Rotherham: Flee fornication! Every sin, whatsoever a man shall commit, is, outside his body, but, he that committeth fornication, bringeth sin into his own body.
text.KJV: 6:18 Flee fornication: Euery sinne that a man doeth, is without the body: but he that committeth fornication, sinneth against his owne body.
text.NABRE: 6:18 Avoid immorality. Every other sin a person commits is outside the body, but the immoral person sins against his own body.

text.NRSV: Or do you not know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit within you, which you have from God, and that you are not your own?
text.Rotherham: Or know ye not that, your body, is, a shrine of the Holy Spirit that is within you, which ye have from God? And ye are not your own;—
text.KJV: 6:19 What, know ye not that your body is the Temple of the holy Ghost which is in you, which yee haue of God, and ye are not your owne?
text.NABRE: 6:19 Do you not know that your body is a temple of the holy Spirit within you, whom you have from God, and that you are not your own?

text.NRSV: For you were bought with a price; therefore glorify God in your body.
text.Rotherham: For ye have been bought with a price! Therefore glorify God in your body.
text.KJV: 6:20 For yee are bought with a price: therefore glorifie God in your body, and in your spirit, which are Gods.
text.NABRE: 6:20 For you have been purchased at a price. Therefore glorify God in your body.

text.NRSV: Now concerning the matters about which you wrote: “It is well for a man not to touch a woman.”
text.Rotherham: Now, concerning the things whereof ye wrote, it were, good, for a man, not to touch, a woman;
text.KJV: 7:1 Now cōcerning the things wherof ye wrote vnto me, It is good for a man not to touch a woman.
text.NABRE: 7:1 III. Answers to the Corinthians’ Questions A. Marriage and Virginity Chapter 7 - Advice to the Married. Now in regard to the matters about which you wrote: “It is a good thing for a man not to touch a woman,”

text.NRSV: But because of cases of sexual immorality, each man should have his own wife and each woman her own husband.
text.Rotherham: But, on account of fornications, let, each man, have, his own wife, and, each woman, have, her own husband:
text.KJV: 7:2 Neuerthelesse, to auoid fornication, let euery man haue his owne wife, and let euery woman haue her owne husband.
text.NABRE: 7:2 but because of cases of immorality every man should have his own wife, and every woman her own husband.

text.NRSV: The husband should give to his wife her conjugal rights, and likewise the wife to her husband.
text.Rotherham: Unto the wife, let the husband render what is her due, and, in like manner, the wife also, unto the husband,—
text.KJV: 7:3 Let the husband render vnto the wife due beneuolence: and likewise also the wife vnto the husband.
text.NABRE: 7:3 The husband should fulfill his duty toward his wife, and likewise the wife toward her husband.

text.NRSV: For the wife does not have authority over her own body, but the husband does; likewise the husband does not have authority over his own body, but the wife does.
text.Rotherham: The wife, over her own body, hath not authority, but the husband, and, in like manner, the husband also, over his own body, hath not authority, but the wife.
text.KJV: 7:4 The wife hath not power of her owne body, but the husband: and likewise also the husband hath not power of his owne body, but the wife.
text.NABRE: 7:4 A wife does not have authority over her own body, but rather her husband, and similarly a husband does not have authority over his own body, but rather his wife.

text.NRSV: Do not deprive one another, except perhaps by agreement for a set time, to devote yourselves to prayer, and then come together again, so that Satan may not tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
text.Rotherham: Be not depriving one another—unless perhaps by consent for a season, that ye may have leisure for prayer, and, again, may be together,—lest Satan be tempting you by reason of your want of self-control.
text.KJV: 7:5 Defraud you not one the other, except it bee with consent for a time, that yee may giue your selues to fasting and prayer, and come together againe, that Satan tempt you not for your incontinencie.
text.NABRE: 7:5 Do not deprive each other, except perhaps by mutual consent for a time, to be free for prayer, but then return to one another, so that Satan may not tempt you through your lack of self-control.

text.NRSV: This I say by way of concession, not of command.
text.Rotherham: This, however I am saying, by way of concession, not of injunction;
text.KJV: 7:6 But I speake this by permission, and not of commandement.
text.NABRE: 7:6 This I say by way of concession, however, not as a command.

text.NRSV: I wish that all were as I myself am. But each has a particular gift from God, one of one kind and one of another.
text.Rotherham: Besides, I desire all men to be, even as myself,—but, each one, hath his, personal, gift from God, one, after this manner, and, another, after that.
text.KJV: 7:7 For I would that all men were euen as I my selfe: but euery man hath his proper gift of God, one after this maner, and another after that.
text.NABRE: 7:7 Indeed, I wish everyone to be as I am, but each has a particular gift from God, one of one kind and one of another.

text.NRSV: To the unmarried and the widows I say that it is well for them to remain unmarried as I am.
text.Rotherham: But I say, to the unmarried, and to the widows, good, were it for them, that they should abide, even as I;
text.KJV: 7:8 I say therefore to the vnmaried and widowes, It is good for them if they abide euen as I.
text.NABRE: 7:8 Now to the unmarried and to widows I say: it is a good thing for them to remain as they are, as I do,

text.NRSV: But if they are not practicing self-control, they should marry. For it is better to marry than to be aflame with passion.
text.Rotherham: But, if they have not self-control, let them marry, for, better, is it, to marry than to burn.
text.KJV: 7:9 But if they cannot conteine, let them marry: for it is better to marrie then to burne.
text.NABRE: 7:9 but if they cannot exercise self-control they should marry, for it is better to marry than to be on fire.

text.NRSV: To the married I give this command—not I but the Lord—that the wife should not separate from her husband
text.Rotherham: To the married, however, I give charge—not, I, but the Lord,—that, a wife, from her husband, do not depart,—
text.KJV: 7:10 And vnto the married, I command, yet not I, but the Lord, Let not the wife depart from her husband:
text.NABRE: 7:10 To the married, however, I give this instruction (not I, but the Lord): A wife should not separate from her husband

text.NRSV: (but if she does separate, let her remain unmarried or else be reconciled to her husband), and that the husband should not divorce his wife.
text.Rotherham: But, if she should even depart, let her remain unmarried, or, to her husband, be reconciled; and let not, a husband, leave, his wife.
text.KJV: 7:11 But and if shee depart, let her remaine vnmaried, or be reconciled to her husband: and let not the husband put away his wife.
text.NABRE: 7:11 —and if she does separate she must either remain single or become reconciled to her husband—and a husband should not divorce his wife.

text.NRSV: To the rest I say—I and not the Lord—that if any believer has a wife who is an unbeliever, and she consents to live with him, he should not divorce her.
text.Rotherham: But, unto the rest, say, I—not the Lord,—if, any brother, hath, a wife that believeth not, and, she, is well pleased to dwell with him, let him not leave her;
text.KJV: 7:12 But to the rest speake I, not the Lord, If any brother hath a wife that beleeueth not, and shee bee pleased to dwell with him, let him not put her away.
text.NABRE: 7:12 To the rest I say (not the Lord): if any brother has a wife who is an unbeliever, and she is willing to go on living with him, he should not divorce her;

text.NRSV: And if any woman has a husband who is an unbeliever, and he consents to live with her, she should not divorce him.
text.Rotherham: And, a woman who hath a husband that believeth not, and, he, is well pleased to dwell with her, let her not leave her husband;—
text.KJV: 7:13 And the woman which hath an husband that beleeueth not, and if hee be pleased to dwell with her, let her not leaue him.
text.NABRE: 7:13 and if any woman has a husband who is an unbeliever, and he is willing to go on living with her, she should not divorce her husband.

text.NRSV: For the unbelieving husband is made holy through his wife, and the unbelieving wife is made holy through her husband. Otherwise, your children would be unclean, but as it is, they are holy.
text.Rotherham: For the husband that believeth not is hallowed in the wife, and the wife that believeth not is hallowed in the brother: else were, your children, impure, but, now, are they, pure.
text.KJV: 7:14 For the vnbeleeuing husband is sanctified by the wife, and the vnbeleeuing wife is sanctified by the husband; else were your children vncleane, but now are they holy.
text.NABRE: 7:14 For the unbelieving husband is made holy through his wife, and the unbelieving wife is made holy through the brother. Otherwise your children would be unclean, whereas in fact they are holy.

text.NRSV: But if the unbelieving partner separates, let it be so; in such a case the brother or sister is not bound. It is to peace that God has called you.
text.Rotherham: But, if, the unbelieving, departeth, let him depart: the brother or the sister hath not come into bondage, in such cases, but, in peace, hath God called us.
text.KJV: 7:15 But if the vnbeleeuing depart, let him depart. A brother or a sister is not vnder bondage in such cases: but God hath called vs to peace.
text.NABRE: 7:15 If the unbeliever separates, however, let him separate. The brother or sister is not bound in such cases; God has called you to peace.

text.NRSV: Wife, for all you know, you might save your husband. Husband, for all you know, you might save your wife.
text.Rotherham: For how knowest thou, O woman, whether, thy husband, thou shalt save? Or how knowest thou, O man, whether, thy wife, thou shalt save?
text.KJV: 7:16 For what knowest thou, O wife, whether thou shalt saue thy husband? or how knowest thou, O man, whether thou shalt saue thy wife?
text.NABRE: 7:16 For how do you know, wife, whether you will save your husband; or how do you know, husband, whether you will save your wife? The Life That the Lord Has Assigned.

text.NRSV: However that may be, let each of you lead the life that the Lord has assigned, to which God has called you. This is my rule in all the churches.
text.Rotherham: If not—as the Lord hath distributed—unto each one, as God hath called, each one, so, let him be walking;—and, so, in all the assemblies, I ordain.
text.KJV: 7:17 But as God hath distributed to euery man, as the Lord hath called euery one, so let him walke, and so ordeine I in all Churches.
text.NABRE: 7:17 Only, everyone should live as the Lord has assigned, just as God called each one. I give this order in all the churches.

text.NRSV: Was anyone at the time of his call already circumcised? Let him not seek to remove the marks of circumcision. Was anyone at the time of his call uncircumcised? Let him not seek circumcision.
text.Rotherham: After being circumcised, was any called? let him not become uncircumcised; in uncircumcision, hath any been called? let him not be circumcised:
text.KJV: 7:18 Is any man called being circumcised? let him not become vncircumcised: Is any called in vncircumcision? let him not be circumcised.
text.NABRE: 7:18 Was someone called after he had been circumcised? He should not try to undo his circumcision. Was an uncircumcised person called? He should not be circumcised.

text.NRSV: Circumcision is nothing, and uncircumcision is nothing; but obeying the commandments of God is everything.
text.Rotherham: The circumcision, is, nothing, and, the uncircumcision, is, nothing,—but keeping the commandments of God.
text.KJV: 7:19 Circumcision is nothing, and vncircumcision is nothing, but the keeping of the Commandements of God.
text.NABRE: 7:19 Circumcision means nothing, and uncircumcision means nothing; what matters is keeping God’s commandments.

text.NRSV: Let each of you remain in the condition in which you were called.
text.Rotherham: Each one, in the calling wherein he was called, in the same, let him abide:
text.KJV: 7:20 Let euery man abide in the same calling wherein he was called.
text.NABRE: 7:20 Everyone should remain in the state in which he was called.

text.NRSV: Were you a slave when called? Do not be concerned about it. Even if you can gain your freedom, make use of your present condition now more than ever.
text.Rotherham: A bond-servant, wast thou called? let it not cause thee concern; but, if thou canst even become, free, rather use it.
text.KJV: 7:21 Art thou called being a seruant? care not for it: but if thou maist be made free, vse it rather.
text.NABRE: 7:21 Were you a slave when you were called? Do not be concerned but, even if you can gain your freedom, make the most of it.

text.NRSV: For whoever was called in the Lord as a slave is a freed person belonging to the Lord, just as whoever was free when called is a slave of Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, he who in the Lord was called, being a bond-servant, is, a freed-man of the Lord: in like manner, he that was called being, a freeman, is Christ’s bond-servant:—
text.KJV: 7:22 For he that is called in the Lord, being a seruant, is the Lords free man: likewise also hee that is called being free, is Christs seruant.
text.NABRE: 7:22 For the slave called in the Lord is a freed person in the Lord, just as the free person who has been called is a slave of Christ.

text.NRSV: You were bought with a price; do not become slaves of human beings.
text.Rotherham: With a price, have ye been bought,—do not become bond-servants of men:
text.KJV: 7:23 Ye are bought with a price, be not ye the seruants of men.
text.NABRE: 7:23 You have been purchased at a price. Do not become slaves to human beings.

text.NRSV: In whatever condition you were called, brothers and sisters, there remain with God.
text.Rotherham: Each one, wherein he was called, brethren, in the same, let him abide with God.
text.KJV: 7:24 Brethren, let euery man wherin he is called, therein abide with God.
text.NABRE: 7:24 Brothers, everyone should continue before God in the state in which he was called.
