text.NRSV: Now concerning virgins, I have no command of the Lord, but I give my opinion as one who by the Lord’s mercy is trustworthy.
text.Rotherham: But, concerning them who are virgin, injunction of the Lord, have I none; yet, a judgment, do I give, as one who hath obtained mercy from the Lord to be, faithful:
text.KJV: 7:25 Nowe concerning virgins, I haue no commaundement of the Lord: yet I giue my iudgement as one that hath obtained mercy of the Lord to be faithfull.
text.NABRE: 7:25 Advice to Virgins and Widows. Now in regard to virgins I have no commandment from the Lord, but I give my opinion as one who by the Lord’s mercy is trustworthy.

text.NRSV: I think that, in view of the impending crisis, it is well for you to remain as you are.
text.Rotherham: I consider this, then, to be, good, in the circumstances, by reason of the existing distress,—that it is, good for a man, so, to be:
text.KJV: 7:26 I suppose therefore that this is good for the present distresse, I say, that it is good for a man so to be.
text.NABRE: 7:26 So this is what I think best because of the present distress: that it is a good thing for a person to remain as he is.

text.NRSV: Are you bound to a wife? Do not seek to be free. Are you free from a wife? Do not seek a wife.
text.Rotherham: Hast thou become bound to a wife? do not seek to be loosed; hast thou become loosed from a wife? do not seek a wife.
text.KJV: 7:27 Art thou bound vnto a wife? seeke not to bee loosed. Art thou loosed from a wife? seeke not a wife.
text.NABRE: 7:27 Are you bound to a wife? Do not seek a separation. Are you free of a wife? Then do not look for a wife.

text.NRSV: But if you marry, you do not sin, and if a virgin marries, she does not sin. Yet those who marry will experience distress in this life, and I would spare you that.
text.Rotherham: If, however, thou shouldst even marry, thou hast not sinned; and, if one who is virgin should marry, that one hath not sinned;—but, tribulation of the flesh, shall, such, have:—howbeit, I, spare you.
text.KJV: 7:28 But and if thou marry, thou hast not sinned, and if a virgin marry, shee hath not sinned: neuerthelesse, such shall haue trouble in the flesh: but I spare you.
text.NABRE: 7:28 If you marry, however, you do not sin, nor does an unmarried woman sin if she marries; but such people will experience affliction in their earthly life, and I would like to spare you that.

text.NRSV: I mean, brothers and sisters, the appointed time has grown short; from now on, let even those who have wives be as though they had none,
text.Rotherham: But, this, I say—the opportunity is, contracted for what remaineth—in order that, they who have wives, may be, as though they had none,
text.KJV: 7:29 But this I say, brethren, the time is short. It remaineth, that both they that haue wiues, be as though they had none:
text.NABRE: 7:29 I tell you, brothers, the time is running out. From now on, let those having wives act as not having them,

text.NRSV: and those who mourn as though they were not mourning, and those who rejoice as though they were not rejoicing, and those who buy as though they had no possessions,
text.Rotherham: And, they who weep, as though they wept not, and, they who rejoice, as though they rejoiced not, and, they who buy, as though they possessed not,
text.KJV: 7:30 And they that weepe, as though they wept not: and they that reioyce, as though they reioyced not: and they that buy, as though they possessed not:
text.NABRE: 7:30 those weeping as not weeping, those rejoicing as not rejoicing, those buying as not owning,

text.NRSV: and those who deal with the world as though they had no dealings with it. For the present form of this world is passing away.
text.Rotherham: And, they who use the world, as though they used it not to the full,—for the fashion of this world passeth away;
text.KJV: 7:31 And they that vse this world, as not abusing it: for the fashion of this world passeth away.
text.NABRE: 7:31 those using the world as not using it fully. For the world in its present form is passing away.

text.NRSV: I want you to be free from anxieties. The unmarried man is anxious about the affairs of the Lord, how to please the Lord;
text.Rotherham: And I desire you to be, without anxiety:—the unmarried man, is anxious for the things of the Lord, how he may please the Lord;
text.KJV: 7:32 But I would haue you without carefulnesse. He that is vnmarried, careth for the things that belōgeth to the Lord, how he may please the Lord:
text.NABRE: 7:32 I should like you to be free of anxieties. An unmarried man is anxious about the things of the Lord, how he may please the Lord.

text.NRSV: but the married man is anxious about the affairs of the world, how to please his wife,
text.Rotherham: But, he that hath married, is anxious for the things of the world, how may please his wife—
text.KJV: 7:33 But hee that is maried, careth for the things that are of the world, how he may please his wife.
text.NABRE: 7:33 But a married man is anxious about the things of the world, how he may please his wife,

text.NRSV: and his interests are divided. And the unmarried woman and the virgin are anxious about the affairs of the Lord, so that they may be holy in body and spirit; but the married woman is anxious about the affairs of the world, how to please her husband.
text.Rotherham: And he is divided; and, the unmarried woman, or the virgin, is anxious for the things of the Lord, that she may be holy [both] in her body and in her spirit; but, she that hath married, is anxious for the things of the world, how she may please her husband.
text.KJV: 7:34 There is difference also between a wife and a virgin: the vnmaried woman careth for the things of the Lord, that shee may be holy, both in body and in spirit: but she that is married, careth for the things of the worlde, how shee may please her husband.
text.NABRE: 7:34 and he is divided. An unmarried woman or a virgin is anxious about the things of the Lord, so that she may be holy in both body and spirit. A married woman, on the other hand, is anxious about the things of the world, how she may please her husband.

text.NRSV: I say this for your own benefit, not to put any restraint upon you, but to promote good order and unhindered devotion to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: This, however, with a view to your own profit, am I saying,—not that, a snare, upon you, I may cast, but with a view to what is comely, and devoted unto the Lord, without distraction.
text.KJV: 7:35 And this I speake for your owne profite, not that I may cast a snare vpon you, but for that which is comely, and that you may attend vpon the Lord without distraction.
text.NABRE: 7:35 I am telling you this for your own benefit, not to impose a restraint upon you, but for the sake of propriety and adherence to the Lord without distraction.

text.NRSV: If anyone thinks that he is not behaving properly toward his fiancée, if his passions are strong, and so it has to be, let him marry as he wishes; it is no sin. Let them marry.
text.Rotherham: If however anyone considereth it behaving unseemly towards his virginity, if he should be beyond the bloom of life,—and, thus, it ought to come about, what he chooseth, let him do,—he sinneth not: let them marry:
text.KJV: 7:36 But if any man thinke that he behaueth himselfe vncomely toward his virgin, if she passe the floure of her age, and neede so require, let him doe what hee will, hee sinneth not: let them marry.
text.NABRE: 7:36 If anyone thinks he is behaving improperly toward his virgin, and if a critical moment has come and so it has to be, let him do as he wishes. He is committing no sin; let them get married.

text.NRSV: But if someone stands firm in his resolve, being under no necessity but having his own desire under control, and has determined in his own mind to keep her as his fiancée, he will do well.
text.Rotherham: But, he that standeth in his heart, steadfast, having no necessity, but hath authority concerning his own will, and, this, hath determined in his own heart,—to preserve his own virginity, well shall he do.
text.KJV: 7:37 Neuerthelesse, hee that standeth stedfast in his heart, hauing no necessitie, but hath power ouer his owne will, and hath so decreed in his heart that he will keepe his virgin, doeth well.
text.NABRE: 7:37 The one who stands firm in his resolve, however, who is not under compulsion but has power over his own will, and has made up his mind to keep his virgin, will be doing well.

text.NRSV: So then, he who marries his fiancée does well; and he who refrains from marriage will do better.
text.Rotherham: So that, he that giveth in marriage his own virginity, doeth, well; and, he that giveth it not, shall do, better.
text.KJV: 7:38 So then he that giueth her in mariage, doeth wel: but he that giueth her not in mariage, doeth better.
text.NABRE: 7:38 So then, the one who marries his virgin does well; the one who does not marry her will do better.

text.NRSV: A wife is bound as long as her husband lives. But if the husband dies, she is free to marry anyone she wishes, only in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: A wife, is bound for as long a time as her husband is living; but, if the husband have fallen asleep, she is, free, to be married unto whom she pleaseth,—only, in the Lord;
text.KJV: 7:39 The wife is bound by the Lawe as long as her husband liueth: but if her husband bee dead, shee is at liberty to bee maried to whom shee will, onely in the Lord.
text.NABRE: 7:39 A wife is bound to her husband as long as he lives. But if her husband dies, she is free to be married to whomever she wishes, provided that it be in the Lord.

text.NRSV: But in my judgment she is more blessed if she remains as she is. And I think that I too have the Spirit of God.
text.Rotherham: But, happier, is she, if, so, she remain,—in my judgment; for I think, I also, have the Spirit of God.
text.KJV: 7:40 But shee is happier if shee sobide, after my iudgment: and I thinke also that I haue the Spirit of God.
text.NABRE: 7:40 She is more blessed, though, in my opinion, if she remains as she is, and I think that I too have the Spirit of God.

text.NRSV: Now concerning food sacrificed to idols: we know that “all of us possess knowledge.” Knowledge puffs up, but love builds up.
text.Rotherham: Now, concerning the idol-sacrifices, we are aware—because we, all, have knowledge,—knowledge, puffeth up, but, love, buildeth up;
text.KJV: 8:1 Now as touching things offered vnto idoles, wee know that wee all haue knowledge. Knowledge puffeth vp: but Charitie edifieth.
text.NABRE: 8:1 B. Offerings to Idols Chapter 8 - Knowledge Insufficient. Now in regard to meat sacrificed to idols: we realize that “all of us have knowledge”; knowledge inflates with pride, but love builds up.

text.NRSV: Anyone who claims to know something does not yet have the necessary knowledge;
text.Rotherham: If anyone thinketh that he knoweth anything, not yet knoweth he, as he must needs come to know,
text.KJV: 8:2 And if any man thinke that hee knoweth any thing, hee knoweth nothing yet as he ought to know.
text.NABRE: 8:2 If anyone supposes he knows something, he does not yet know as he ought to know.

text.NRSV: but anyone who loves God is known by him.
text.Rotherham: But, if anyone loveth God, the same, is known of him—
text.KJV: 8:3 But if any man loue God, the same is knowen of him.
text.NABRE: 8:3 But if one loves God, one is known by him.

text.NRSV: Hence, as to the eating of food offered to idols, we know that “no idol in the world really exists,” and that “there is no God but one.”
text.Rotherham: Concerning, then, the eating of idol-sacrifices, we are aware—that an idol is, nothing, in the world, and that, none, is God save one.
text.KJV: 8:4 As concerning therefore the eating of those things that are offered in sacrifice vnto idoles, wee know that an idole is nothing in the world, and that there is none other God but one.
text.NABRE: 8:4 So about the eating of meat sacrificed to idols: we know that “there is no idol in the world,” and that “there is no God but one.”

text.NRSV: Indeed, even though there may be so-called gods in heaven or on earth—as in fact there are many gods and many lords—
text.Rotherham: For, if indeed there are so-called gods, whether in heaven or upon earth,—as indeed there are gods many and lords many,
text.KJV: 8:5 For though there bee that are called gods, whether in heauen or in earth (as there be gods many, and lords many:)
text.NABRE: 8:5 Indeed, even though there are so-called gods in heaven and on earth (there are, to be sure, many “gods” and many “lords”),

text.NRSV: yet for us there is one God, the Father, from whom are all things and for whom we exist, and one Lord, Jesus Christ, through whom are all things and through whom we exist.
text.Rotherham: [Yet], to us, there is one God the Father, of whom are all things, and, we, for him; and one Lord Jesus Christ, through whom are all things, and, we, through him.
text.KJV: 8:6 But to vs there is but one God, the Father, of whom are all things, and we in him, and one Lord Iesus Christ, by whom are all things, and we by him.
text.NABRE: 8:6 yet for us there is one God, the Father, from whom all things are and for whom we exist, and one Lord, Jesus Christ, through whom all things are and through whom we exist.

text.NRSV: It is not everyone, however, who has this knowledge. Since some have become so accustomed to idols until now, they still think of the food they eat as food offered to an idol; and their conscience, being weak, is defiled.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, not in all, is the knowledge; but, some, by their familiarity, until even now, with the idol, as an idol-sacrifice, eat it, and, their conscience, being, weak, is defiled.
text.KJV: 8:7 Howbeit there is not in euerie man that knowledge: for some with conscience of the idole vnto this houre, eate it as a thing offred vnto an idole, and their conscience being weake, is defiled.
text.NABRE: 8:7 Practical Rules. But not all have this knowledge. There are some who have been so used to idolatry up until now that, when they eat meat sacrificed to idols, their conscience, which is weak, is defiled.

text.NRSV: “Food will not bring us close to God.” We are no worse off if we do not eat, and no better off if we do.
text.Rotherham: Food, indeed, will not commend us unto God,—neither, if we eat not, do we lack, nor, if we eat, do we abound.
text.KJV: 8:8 But meate commendeth vs not to God: for neither if we eate, are we the better: neither if wee eate not, are we the worse.
text.NABRE: 8:8 Now food will not bring us closer to God. We are no worse off if we do not eat, nor are we better off if we do.

text.NRSV: But take care that this liberty of yours does not somehow become a stumbling block to the weak.
text.Rotherham: But be taking heed, lest, by any means, your right, itself, become, an occasion of stumbling, unto the weak;
text.KJV: 8:9 But take heed lest by any meanes, this libertie of yours become a stumbling blocke to them that are weake.
text.NABRE: 8:9 But make sure that this liberty of yours in no way becomes a stumbling block to the weak.

text.NRSV: For if others see you, who possess knowledge, eating in the temple of an idol, might they not, since their conscience is weak, be encouraged to the point of eating food sacrificed to idols?
text.Rotherham: For, if anyone should see [thee] who hast knowledge, in an idol-temple, reclining, will not, his conscience, being, weak, be built up for the eating of the idol-sacrifices?
text.KJV: 8:10 For if any man see thee which hast knowledge, sit at meat in the idols temple: shall not the conscience of him which is weake, be emboldened to eat those things which are offered to idols?
text.NABRE: 8:10 If someone sees you, with your knowledge, reclining at table in the temple of an idol, may not his conscience too, weak as it is, be “built up” to eat the meat sacrificed to idols?

text.NRSV: So by your knowledge those weak believers for whom Christ died are destroyed.
text.Rotherham: In fact, he that is weak, is being destroyed, by thy knowledge—the brother for whose sake Christ died.
text.KJV: 8:11 And through thy knowledge shal the weake brother perish, for whome Christ died?
text.NABRE: 8:11 Thus through your knowledge, the weak person is brought to destruction, the brother for whom Christ died.

text.NRSV: But when you thus sin against members of your family, and wound their conscience when it is weak, you sin against Christ.
text.Rotherham: But, in thus sinning against the brethren am wounding their conscience, seeing it is weak, against Christ, are ye sinning.
text.KJV: 8:12 But when ye sinne so against the brethren, and wound their weake conscience, ye sinne against Christ.
text.NABRE: 8:12 When you sin in this way against your brothers and wound their consciences, weak as they are, you are sinning against Christ.

text.NRSV: Therefore, if food causes my brother or sister to fall, I will never eat meat, so that I may not cause one of them to fall.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, if food is an occasion of stumbling unto my brother, in nowise will I eat flesh unto the age that abideth,—that, I may not occasion, my brother, to stumble.
text.KJV: 8:13 Wherefore if meate make my brother to offend, I will eat no flesh while the world standeth, lest I make my brother to offend.
text.NABRE: 8:13 Therefore, if food causes my brother to sin, I will never eat meat again, so that I may not cause my brother to sin.

text.NRSV: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are you not my work in the Lord?
text.Rotherham: Am not free? Am I not an apostle? Jesus our Lord, have I not seen? Are not, ye, my work, in the Lord?
text.KJV: 9:1 Am I not an Apostle? am I not free? haue I not seene Iesus Christ our Lord? Are not you my worke in the Lord?
text.NABRE: 9:1 Chapter 9 - Paul’s Rights as an Apostle. Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are you not my work in the Lord?

text.NRSV: If I am not an apostle to others, at least I am to you; for you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: If, unto others, I am not an apostle, certainly at least, unto you, I am; for, the seal of my apostleship, ye, are in the Lord.
text.KJV: 9:2 If I bee not an Apostle vnto others, yet doubtlesse I am to you: for the seale of mine Apostleship are yee in the Lord.
text.NABRE: 9:2 Although I may not be an apostle for others, certainly I am for you, for you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.

text.NRSV: This is my defense to those who would examine me.
text.Rotherham: My defence, unto them who are examining me, is this:
text.KJV: 9:3 Mine answere to them that doe examine me, is this:
text.NABRE: 9:3 My defense against those who would pass judgment on me is this.

text.NRSV: Do we not have the right to our food and drink?
text.Rotherham: Have we not a right to eat and drink?
text.KJV: 9:4 Haue wee not power to eate and to drinke?
text.NABRE: 9:4 Do we not have the right to eat and drink?

text.NRSV: Do we not have the right to be accompanied by a believing wife, as do the other apostles and the brothers of the Lord and Cephas?
text.Rotherham: Have we not a right to take round, a sister wife,—as even the rest of the apostles, and the brethren of the Lord, and Cephas?
text.KJV: 9:5 Haue we not power to lead about a sister a wife aswel as other Apostles, and as the brethren of the Lord, and Cephas?
text.NABRE: 9:5 Do we not have the right to take along a Christian wife, as do the rest of the apostles, and the brothers of the Lord, and Cephas?

text.NRSV: Or is it only Barnabas and I who have no right to refrain from working for a living?
text.Rotherham: Or have, only I and Barnabas, not a right to forbear working?
text.KJV: 9:6 Or I onely and Barnabas, haue not we power to forbeare working?
text.NABRE: 9:6 Or is it only myself and Barnabas who do not have the right not to work?

text.NRSV: Who at any time pays the cost of his own military service? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat any of its fruit? Or who tends a flock and does not get any of its milk?
text.Rotherham: Who serveth as a soldier, at his own charges, at any time? Who planteth a vineyard,—and, the fruit thereof, doth not eat? [Or] who shepherdeth a flock,—and, of the milk of the flock, doth not eat?
text.KJV: 9:7 Who goeth a warfare any time at his owne charges? who planteth a vineyard, and eateth not of the fruite thereof? or who feedeth a flocke, and eateth not of the milke of the flocke?
text.NABRE: 9:7 Who ever serves as a soldier at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard without eating its produce? Or who shepherds a flock without using some of the milk from the flock?

text.NRSV: Do I say this on human authority? Does not the law also say the same?
text.Rotherham: Is it, after the manner of men, that these things I am saying? Or doth not, even the law, the same things, say?
text.KJV: 9:8 Say I these things as a man? or saith not the Law the same also?
text.NABRE: 9:8 Am I saying this on human authority, or does not the law also speak of these things?

text.NRSV: For it is written in the law of Moses, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain.” Is it for oxen that God is concerned?
text.Rotherham: For, in the law of Moses, it is written—Thou shalt not muzzle an ox when it is treading out the corn:—Is it, for the oxen, God is caring?
text.KJV: 9:9 For it is written in the Law of Moyses, Thou shalt not muzzell the mouth of the oxe that treadeth out the corne: doth God take care for oxen?
text.NABRE: 9:9 It is written in the law of Moses, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain.” Is God concerned about oxen,

text.NRSV: Or does he not speak entirely for our sake? It was indeed written for our sake, for the plowman should plow in hope and the thresher thresh in hope of a share in the crop.
text.Rotherham: Or, for our sakes altogether, is he saying it? For our sakes, it was written; because, he that ploweth, ought, to plow, in hope,—and, he that thresheth, [to thresh] in hope of partaking.
text.KJV: 9:10 Or saith hee it altogether for our sakes? for our sakes, no doubt, this is written: that hee that ploweth, should plow in hope: and that hee that thresheth in hope, should bee partaker of his hope.
text.NABRE: 9:10 or is he not really speaking for our sake? It was written for our sake, because the plowman should plow in hope, and the thresher in hope of receiving a share.

text.NRSV: If we have sown spiritual good among you, is it too much if we reap your material benefits?
text.Rotherham: If, we, unto you, the things of the Spirit have sown, is it a great matter, if, we, of you, the things of the flesh shall reap?
text.KJV: 9:11 If we haue sowen vnto you spirituall things, is it a great thing if wee shall reape your carnall things?
text.NABRE: 9:11 If we have sown spiritual seed for you, is it a great thing that we reap a material harvest from you?

text.NRSV: If others share this rightful claim on you, do not we still more? Nevertheless, we have not made use of this right, but we endure anything rather than put an obstacle in the way of the gospel of Christ.
text.Rotherham: If, others, of this right, over you, are partaking, [should] not rather, we? nevertheless, we used not this right; but, all [such] things, do we conceal,—lest we should cause any hindrance unto the glad message of the Christ.
text.KJV: 9:12 If others bee partakers of this power ouer you, are not we rather? Neuerthelesse, we haue not vsed this power: but suffer all things, lest wee should hinder the Gospel of Christ.
text.NABRE: 9:12 If others share this rightful claim on you, do not we still more? Reason for Not Using His Rights. Yet we have not used this right. On the contrary, we endure everything so as not to place an obstacle to the gospel of Christ.

text.NRSV: Do you not know that those who are employed in the temple service get their food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share in what is sacrificed on the altar?
text.Rotherham: Know ye not that, they who labour in the holy rites, do eat the provisions out of the holy place? They who at the altar wait, do, with the altar, share?
text.KJV: 9:13 Do ye not know that they which minister about holy things, liue of the things of the Temple? and they which wait at the altar, are partakers with the altar?
text.NABRE: 9:13 Do you not know that those who perform the temple services eat [what] belongs to the temple, and those who minister at the altar share in the sacrificial offerings?

text.NRSV: In the same way, the Lord commanded that those who proclaim the gospel should get their living by the gospel.
text.Rotherham: Thus, also, hath the Lord, appointed—that, they who the glad message tell, should, of the glad-message, live.
text.KJV: 9:14 Euen so hath the Lord ordeined, that they which preach the Gospel, should liue of the Gospel.
text.NABRE: 9:14 In the same way, the Lord ordered that those who preach the gospel should live by the gospel.

text.NRSV: But I have made no use of any of these rights, nor am I writing this so that they may be applied in my case. Indeed, I would rather die than that—no one will deprive me of my ground for boasting!
text.Rotherham: I, however, have not used any of these things; and have not written these things, in order that, so it should be done, in my case; for it were, good for me, rather to die than——my boast, shall, no man, make void!
text.KJV: 9:15 But I haue vsed none of these things. Neither haue I written these things, that it should bee so done vnto me: for it were better for me to die, then that any man should make my glorying voyd.
text.NABRE: 9:15 I have not used any of these rights, however, nor do I write this that it be done so in my case. I would rather die. Certainly no one is going to nullify my boast.

text.NRSV: If I proclaim the gospel, this gives me no ground for boasting, for an obligation is laid on me, and woe to me if I do not proclaim the gospel!
text.Rotherham: For, if I be telling the glad-message, it is, with me, no matter of boasting; for, necessity, upon me, lieth,—for it is, Woe to me, if I should not be telling the glad-message;
text.KJV: 9:16 For though I preach the Gospel, I haue nothing to glorie of: for necessitie is laid vpon mee, yea, woe is vnto me, if I preach not the Gospel.
text.NABRE: 9:16 If I preach the gospel, this is no reason for me to boast, for an obligation has been imposed on me, and woe to me if I do not preach it!

text.NRSV: For if I do this of my own will, I have a reward; but if not of my own will, I am entrusted with a commission.
text.Rotherham: For, if, by choice, this thing I am doing, I have, a reward; but, if not by choice, with a stewardship, have I been entrusted!
text.KJV: 9:17 For if I doe this thing willingly, I haue a reward: but if against my will, a dispensation of the Gospel is committed vnto me.
text.NABRE: 9:17 If I do so willingly, I have a recompense, but if unwillingly, then I have been entrusted with a stewardship.

text.NRSV: What then is my reward? Just this: that in my proclamation I may make the gospel free of charge, so as not to make full use of my rights in the gospel.
text.Rotherham: What, then, is my reward? that, in telling the glad-message, free of cost, I should put the glad-message,—to the end I should not use to the full my right in the glad-message.
text.KJV: 9:18 What is my reward then? verily that when I preach the Gospel, I may make the Gospel of Christ without charge, that I abuse not my power in the Gospel.
text.NABRE: 9:18 What then is my recompense? That, when I preach, I offer the gospel free of charge so as not to make full use of my right in the gospel.

text.NRSV: For though I am free with respect to all, I have made myself a slave to all, so that I might win more of them.
text.Rotherham: For, though free from all, unto all, myself, I enslaved,—that, the larger number, I might win:
text.KJV: 9:19 For though I bee free from all men, yet haue I made my selfe seruant vnto all, that I might gaine the more.
text.NABRE: 9:19 All Things to All. Although I am free in regard to all, I have made myself a slave to all so as to win over as many as possible.

text.NRSV: To the Jews I became as a Jew, in order to win Jews. To those under the law I became as one under the law (though I myself am not under the law) so that I might win those under the law.
text.Rotherham: Therefore became I, to the Jews, as, a Jew,—that, Jews, I might win; to them who were under law, as, under law, not being, myself, under law,—that, them who were under law, I might win;
text.KJV: 9:20 And vnto the Iewes, I became as a Iew, that I might gaine the Iewes: to them that are vnder the Law, as vnder the Law, that I might gaine them that are vnder the Law:
text.NABRE: 9:20 To the Jews I became like a Jew to win over Jews; to those under the law I became like one under the law—though I myself am not under the law—to win over those under the law.

text.NRSV: To those outside the law I became as one outside the law (though I am not free from God’s law but am under Christ’s law) so that I might win those outside the law.
text.Rotherham: To them who were without law, as, without law,—not being without law to God, but lawfully subject to Christ,—that I might win them who were without law.
text.KJV: 9:21 To them that are without Law, as without Law (being not without Law to God, but vnder the Law to Christ,) that I might gaine them that are without Law.
text.NABRE: 9:21 To those outside the law I became like one outside the law—though I am not outside God’s law but within the law of Christ—to win over those outside the law.

text.NRSV: To the weak I became weak, so that I might win the weak. I have become all things to all people, that I might by all means save some.
text.Rotherham: I became, unto the weak, weak,—that, the weak, I might win;—to all men, have I become all things, that, by all means, some, I might save.
text.KJV: 9:22 To the weake became I as weake, that I might gaine the weake: I am made all things to all men, that I might by all meanes saue some.
text.NABRE: 9:22 To the weak I became weak, to win over the weak. I have become all things to all, to save at least some.

text.NRSV: I do it all for the sake of the gospel, so that I may share in its blessings.
text.Rotherham: But, all things, am I doing, for the sake of the glad-message, that a joint-partaker thereof, I may become.
text.KJV: 9:23 And this I doe for the Gospels sake, that I might be partaker thereof with you.
text.NABRE: 9:23 All this I do for the sake of the gospel, so that I too may have a share in it.

text.NRSV: Do you not know that in a race the runners all compete, but only one receives the prize? Run in such a way that you may win it.
text.Rotherham: Know ye not that, they who, in a racecourse, run, all, indeed, run,—but, one, receiveth the prize? So, be running, that ye may lay hold.
text.KJV: 9:24 Know yee not that they which runne in a race, runne all, but one receiueth the price? So runne, that yee may obtaine.
text.NABRE: 9:24 Do you not know that the runners in the stadium all run in the race, but only one wins the prize? Run so as to win.

text.NRSV: Athletes exercise self-control in all things; they do it to receive a perishable wreath, but we an imperishable one.
text.Rotherham: But, every man who striveth in the games, in all things, useth self-control;—they, indeed, then, that a corruptible crown, they may receive; but, we, an incorruptible!
text.KJV: 9:25 And euery man that striueth for the masterie, is temperate in all things: Now they doe it to obtaine a corruptible crowne, but we an incorruptible.
text.NABRE: 9:25 Every athlete exercises discipline in every way. They do it to win a perishable crown, but we an imperishable one.

text.NRSV: So I do not run aimlessly, nor do I box as though beating the air;
text.Rotherham: I, therefore, so, am running, as, not uncertainly, so, am boxing, as not thrashing air;
text.KJV: 9:26 I therefore so runne, not as vncertainely: so fight I, not as one that beateth the ayre:
text.NABRE: 9:26 Thus I do not run aimlessly; I do not fight as if I were shadowboxing.

text.NRSV: but I punish my body and enslave it, so that after proclaiming to others I myself should not be disqualified.
text.Rotherham: But am beating my body under, and leading it captive, lest, by any means,—unto others, having proclaimed, [the contest], I myself, should be rejected.
text.KJV: 9:27 But I keepe vnder my body, and bring it into subiection: lest that by any meanes when I haue preached to others, I my selfe should be a castaway.
text.NABRE: 9:27 No, I drive my body and train it, for fear that, after having preached to others, I myself should be disqualified.

text.NRSV: I do not want you to be unaware, brothers and sisters, that our ancestors were all under the cloud, and all passed through the sea,
text.Rotherham: For I wish not ye should be ignorant, brethren, that, all our fathers, were, under the cloud, and, all, passed through the sea,—
text.KJV: 10:1 Moreouer brethren, I would not that yee should be ignorant, how that all our fathers were vnder the cloud, and all passed thorow the Sea:
text.NABRE: 10:1 Chapter 10 - Warning Against Overconfidence. I do not want you to be unaware, brothers, that our ancestors were all under the cloud and all passed through the sea,

text.NRSV: and all were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea,
text.Rotherham: And, all, immersed themselves into Moses, in the cloud, and in the sea;
text.KJV: 10:2 And were all baptized vnto Moyses in the cloud, and in the sea:
text.NABRE: 10:2 and all of them were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.

text.NRSV: and all ate the same spiritual food,
text.Rotherham: And, all, did eat [the same] spiritual food,
text.KJV: 10:3 And did all eat the same spirituall meat:
text.NABRE: 10:3 All ate the same spiritual food,

text.NRSV: and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank from the spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
text.Rotherham: And, all, drank, the same spiritual drink,—for they continued to drink of the spiritual rock that followed them, and, the rock, was the Christ:—
text.KJV: 10:4 And did all drinke the same spirituall drinke: (for they dranke of that spirituall Rocke that followed them: and that Rocke was Christ)
text.NABRE: 10:4 and all drank the same spiritual drink, for they drank from a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was the Christ.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless, God was not pleased with most of them, and they were struck down in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, with the most of them, God, was not well-pleased, for they were strewed along in the desert.
text.KJV: 10:5 But with many of them God was not well pleased: for they were ouerthrowen in the wildernesse.
text.NABRE: 10:5 Yet God was not pleased with most of them, for they were struck down in the desert.

text.NRSV: Now these things occurred as examples for us, so that we might not desire evil as they did.
text.Rotherham: But, in these things, they became, ensamples for us, to the end we should not be covetous of evil things, even as, they, also coveted;
text.KJV: 10:6 Now these things were our examples, to the intent wee should not lust after euil things, as they also lusted.
text.NABRE: 10:6 These things happened as examples for us, so that we might not desire evil things, as they did.

text.NRSV: Do not become idolaters as some of them did; as it is written, “The people sat down to eat and drink, and they rose up to play.”
text.Rotherham: Neither become ye, idolaters, as some of them,—as it is written—The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to be making sport;
text.KJV: 10:7 Neither be ye idolaters, as were some of them, as it is written, The people sate downe to eate and drinke, and rose vp to play.
text.NABRE: 10:7 And do not become idolaters, as some of them did, as it is written, “The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to revel.”

text.NRSV: We must not indulge in sexual immorality as some of them did, and twenty-three thousand fell in a single day.
text.Rotherham: Neither let us commit fornication, as, some of them, committed fornication, and there fell, in one day, three and twenty thousand;
text.KJV: 10:8 Neither let vs commit fornication, as some of them committed, and fell in one day three and twentie thousand.
text.NABRE: 10:8 Let us not indulge in immorality as some of them did, and twenty-three thousand fell within a single day.

text.NRSV: We must not put Christ to the test, as some of them did, and were destroyed by serpents.
text.Rotherham: Neither let us be putting the Lord to the test, as, some of them, put him to the test, and, by the serpents, were perishing;
text.KJV: 10:9 Neither let vs tempt Christ, as some of them also tempted, and were destroyed of serpents.
text.NABRE: 10:9 Let us not test Christ as some of them did, and suffered death by serpents.

text.NRSV: And do not complain as some of them did, and were destroyed by the destroyer.
text.Rotherham: Neither be ye murmuring, even, as some of them, murmured, and perished by the destroyer.
text.KJV: 10:10 Neither murmure ye, as some of them also murmured, and were destroyed of the destroyer.
text.NABRE: 10:10 Do not grumble as some of them did, and suffered death by the destroyer.

text.NRSV: These things happened to them to serve as an example, and they were written down to instruct us, on whom the ends of the ages have come.
text.Rotherham: But, these things, by way of type, were happening unto them, and were written with a view to our admonition, unto whom, the ends of the ages, have reached along.
text.KJV: 10:11 Now all these things happened vnto them for ensamples: and they are written for our admonition, vpon whom the ends of the world are come.
text.NABRE: 10:11 These things happened to them as an example, and they have been written down as a warning to us, upon whom the end of the ages has come.

text.NRSV: So if you think you are standing, watch out that you do not fall.
text.Rotherham: So that, he that thinketh he is standing, let him take heed lest he fall.
text.KJV: 10:12 Wherefore, let him that thinketh he standeth, take heed lest he fall.
text.NABRE: 10:12 Therefore, whoever thinks he is standing secure should take care not to fall.

text.NRSV: No testing has overtaken you that is not common to everyone. God is faithful, and he will not let you be tested beyond your strength, but with the testing he will also provide the way out so that you may be able to endure it.
text.Rotherham: Trial, hath not taken you, save such as man can bear; faithful, moreover is God, who will not suffer you to be tried above what ye are able, but will make, with the trial, also the way of escape, that ye may be able to hold out.
text.KJV: 10:13 There hath no temptation taken you, but such as is common to man: but God is faithfull, who wil not suffer you to bee tempted aboue that you are able: but will with the temptation also make a way to escape, that ye may bee able to beare it.
text.NABRE: 10:13 No trial has come to you but what is human. God is faithful and will not let you be tried beyond your strength; but with the trial he will also provide a way out, so that you may be able to bear it. Warning Against Idolatry.

text.NRSV: Therefore, my dear friends, flee from the worship of idols.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry,—
text.KJV: 10:14 Wherefore my dearely beloued, flee from idolatrie.
text.NABRE: 10:14 Therefore, my beloved, avoid idolatry.

text.NRSV: I speak as to sensible people; judge for yourselves what I say.
text.Rotherham: As, to prudent men, I speak,—judge, ye, what I say:—
text.KJV: 10:15 I speake as to wise men: iudge ye what I say.
text.NABRE: 10:15 I am speaking as to sensible people; judge for yourselves what I am saying.

text.NRSV: The cup of blessing that we bless, is it not a sharing in the blood of Christ? The bread that we break, is it not a sharing in the body of Christ?
text.Rotherham: The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not, a sharing together of the blood of the Christ? The loaf which we break, is it not, a sharing together of the body of the Christ?
text.KJV: 10:16 The cup of blessing which wee blesse, is it not the communion of the blood of Christ? The bread which we breake, is it not the communion of the body of Christ?
text.NABRE: 10:16 The cup of blessing that we bless, is it not a participation in the blood of Christ? The bread that we break, is it not a participation in the body of Christ?

text.NRSV: Because there is one bread, we who are many are one body, for we all partake of the one bread.
text.Rotherham: Because, one loaf, one body, we, the many, are, for we, all, of the one loaf, partake.
text.KJV: 10:17 For we being many are one bread, and one body: for we are all partakers of that one bread.
text.NABRE: 10:17 Because the loaf of bread is one, we, though many, are one body, for we all partake of the one loaf.

text.NRSV: Consider the people of Israel; are not those who eat the sacrifices partners in the altar?
text.Rotherham: Be looking at Israel after the flesh:—are not, they who eat the sacrifices, joint partakers with the altar?
text.KJV: 10:18 Behold Israel after the flesh: are not they which eat of the sacrifices, partakers of the Altar?
text.NABRE: 10:18 Look at Israel according to the flesh; are not those who eat the sacrifices participants in the altar?

text.NRSV: What do I imply then? That food sacrificed to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?
text.Rotherham: What, then, am I saying?—that, an idol-sacrifice, is anything? or that, an idol, is anything?
text.KJV: 10:19 What say I then? that the idole is any thing? or that which is offered in sacrifice to idols is any thing?
text.NABRE: 10:19 So what am I saying? That meat sacrificed to idols is anything? Or that an idol is anything?

text.NRSV: No, I imply that what pagans sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons and not to God. I do not want you to be partners with demons.
text.Rotherham: On the contrary—that, the things which the nations sacrifice, unto demons, and not unto God, they sacrifice; and I wish not that ye should become, sharers together with the demons!
text.KJV: 10:20 But I say that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to deuils, and not to God: and I would not that yee should haue fellowship with deuils.
text.NABRE: 10:20 No, I mean that what they sacrifice, [they sacrifice] to demons, not to God, and I do not want you to become participants with demons.

text.NRSV: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You cannot partake of the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
text.Rotherham: Ye cannot be drinking—a cup of the Lord, and a cup of demons; ye cannot be partaking of, a table of the Lord, and a table of demons.
text.KJV: 10:21 Yee cannot drinke the cup of the Lord, and the cup of deuils: ye cannot be partakers of the Lords Table, and of the table of deuils.
text.NABRE: 10:21 You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and also the cup of demons. You cannot partake of the table of the Lord and of the table of demons.

text.NRSV: Or are we provoking the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he?
text.Rotherham: Or are we to provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we mightier than he?
text.KJV: 10:22 Doe we prouoke the Lord to iealousie? are we stronger then he?
text.NABRE: 10:22 Or are we provoking the Lord to jealous anger? Are we stronger than he? Seek the Good of Others.

text.NRSV: “All things are lawful,” but not all things are beneficial. “All things are lawful,” but not all things build up.
text.Rotherham: All things are allowable—but, not all things, are profitable,—all things are allowable—but, not all things, upbuild.
text.KJV: 10:23 All things are lawfull for me, but all things are not expedient: All things are lawfull for mee, but all things edifie not.
text.NABRE: 10:23 “Everything is lawful,” but not everything is beneficial. “Everything is lawful,” but not everything builds up.

text.NRSV: Do not seek your own advantage, but that of others.
text.Rotherham: Let, no one, be seeking, what concerneth himself, but that which concerneth some one else.
text.KJV: 10:24 Let no man seeke his owne: but euery man anothers wealth.
text.NABRE: 10:24 No one should seek his own advantage, but that of his neighbor.

text.NRSV: Eat whatever is sold in the meat market without raising any question on the ground of conscience,
text.Rotherham: Whatsoever, in the market, is sold, eat,—asking no question, for conscience sake;
text.KJV: 10:25 Whatsoeuer is solde in the shambles, that eate, asking no question for conscience sake.
text.NABRE: 10:25 Eat anything sold in the market, without raising questions on grounds of conscience,

text.NRSV: for “the earth and its fullness are the Lord’s.”
text.Rotherham: For, unto the Lord, belongeth the earth, and the fullness thereof.
text.KJV: 10:26 For the earth is the Lords, and the fulnesse thereof.
text.NABRE: 10:26 for “the earth and its fullness are the Lord’s.”

text.NRSV: If an unbeliever invites you to a meal and you are disposed to go, eat whatever is set before you without raising any question on the ground of conscience.
text.Rotherham: If one of them who believe not invite you, and ye are disposed to go, whatsoever is set before you, eat,—asking no question, for conscience sake.
text.KJV: 10:27 If any of them that beleeue not, bid you to a feast, and yee be disposed to goe, whatsoeuer is set before you, eate, asking no question for conscience sake.
text.NABRE: 10:27 If an unbeliever invites you and you want to go, eat whatever is placed before you, without raising questions on grounds of conscience.

text.NRSV: But if someone says to you, “This has been offered in sacrifice,” then do not eat it, out of consideration for the one who informed you, and for the sake of conscience—
text.Rotherham: But, if anyone should say unto you,—This, is, a holy sacrifice, do not eat, for that man’s sake who disclosed it, and for conscience sake:—
text.KJV: 10:28 But if any man say vnto you, This is offered in sacrifice vnto idoles, eate not for his sake that shewed it, and for conscience sake. The earth is the Lords, and the fulnesse thereof.
text.NABRE: 10:28 But if someone says to you, “This was offered in sacrifice,” do not eat it on account of the one who called attention to it and on account of conscience;

text.NRSV: I mean the other’s conscience, not your own. For why should my liberty be subject to the judgment of someone else’s conscience?
text.Rotherham: But, conscience, I mean, not thine own, but the other’s,—for why is my freedom to be judged by another’s conscience?
text.KJV: 10:29 Conscience I say, not thine owne, but of the others: for why is my libertie iudged of another mans conscience?
text.NABRE: 10:29 I mean not your own conscience, but the other’s. For why should my freedom be determined by someone else’s conscience?

text.NRSV: If I partake with thankfulness, why should I be reviled because of something for which I give thanks?
text.Rotherham: If, I, with gratitude, partake, why am I to be defamed, as to that for which, I, give thanks?
text.KJV: 10:30 For, if I by grace be a partaker, why am I euill spoken of, for that for which I giue thankes?
text.NABRE: 10:30 If I partake thankfully, why am I reviled for that over which I give thanks?

text.NRSV: So, whether you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do everything for the glory of God.
text.Rotherham: Whether, therefore, ye eat or drink, or whatsoever ye do, do, all things to the glory of God.
text.KJV: 10:31 Whether therfore ye eat or drinke, or whatsoeuer ye doe, doe all to the glory of God.
text.NABRE: 10:31 So whether you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do everything for the glory of God.

text.NRSV: Give no offense to Jews or to Greeks or to the church of God,
text.Rotherham: Give no occasion of stumbling—whether to Jews, or to Greeks, or to the assembly of God:
text.KJV: 10:32 Giue none offence, neither to the Iewes, nor to the Gentiles, nor to the Church of God:
text.NABRE: 10:32 Avoid giving offense, whether to Jews or Greeks or the church of God,

text.NRSV: just as I try to please everyone in everything I do, not seeking my own advantage, but that of many, so that they may be saved.
text.Rotherham: Even as, I also, in all things do please all, not seeking my own profit, but that of the many, that they may be saved.
text.KJV: 10:33 Euen as I please all men in all things, not seeking mine owne profit, but the profit of many, that they may be saued.
text.NABRE: 10:33 just as I try to please everyone in every way, not seeking my own benefit but that of the many, that they may be saved.

text.NRSV: Be imitators of me, as I am of Christ.
text.Rotherham: Become imitators of me,—even as, I also, [am] of Christ.
text.KJV: 11:1 Be yee followers of mee, euen as I also am of Christ.
text.NABRE: 11:1 Chapter 11 - Be imitators of me, as I am of Christ.

text.NRSV: I commend you because you remember me in everything and maintain the traditions just as I handed them on to you.
text.Rotherham: Now I praise you, that, in all things, ye have me in remembrance, and, even as I delivered unto you the instructions, ye hold them fast.
text.KJV: 11:2 Now I prayse you, brethren, that you remember me in all things, and keepe the ordinances, as I deliuered them to you.
text.NABRE: 11:2 IV. Problems in Liturgical Assemblies - I praise you because you remember me in everything and hold fast to the traditions, just as I handed them on to you.

text.NRSV: But I want you to understand that Christ is the head of every man, and the husband is the head of his wife, and God is the head of Christ.
text.Rotherham: But I wish you to know—that, the head of every man, is, the Christ, and, the head of a woman, is, the man; and, the head of the Christ, is, God.
text.KJV: 11:3 But I would haue you knowe, that the head of euery man is Christ: and the head of the woman is the man, and the head of Christ is God.
text.NABRE: 11:3 A. Women’s Headdresses - Man and Woman. But I want you to know that Christ is the head of every man, and a husband the head of his wife, and God the head of Christ.

text.NRSV: Any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head disgraces his head,
text.Rotherham: Every man, praying, or prophesying, having anything upon his head, putteth to shame his head;
text.KJV: 11:4 Euery man praying or prophecying, hauing his head couered, dishonoureth his head.
text.NABRE: 11:4 Any man who prays or prophesies with his head covered brings shame upon his head.

text.NRSV: but any woman who prays or prophesies with her head unveiled disgraces her head—it is one and the same thing as having her head shaved.
text.Rotherham: But, every woman, praying, or prophesying, with her head, unveiled, putteth to shame her head,—for it is, one and the same, with her having been shaven.
text.KJV: 11:5 But euery woman that prayeth or prophesieth with her head vncouered, dishonoureth her head: for that is euen all one as if she were shauen.
text.NABRE: 11:5 But any woman who prays or prophesies with her head unveiled brings shame upon her head, for it is one and the same thing as if she had had her head shaved.

text.NRSV: For if a woman will not veil herself, then she should cut off her hair; but if it is disgraceful for a woman to have her hair cut off or she should be shaved, she should wear a veil.
text.Rotherham: For, if a woman doth not veil herself, let her also be shorn; but, if it were a shame in a woman to be shorn or shaven, let her be veiled.
text.KJV: 11:6 For if the woman be not couered, let her also bee shorne: but if it bee a shame for a woman to be shorne or shauen, let her be couered.
text.NABRE: 11:6 For if a woman does not have her head veiled, she may as well have her hair cut off. But if it is shameful for a woman to have her hair cut off or her head shaved, then she should wear a veil.

text.NRSV: For a man ought not to have his head veiled, since he is the image and reflection of God; but woman is the reflection of man.
text.Rotherham: For, a man, indeed, ought not to be veiling his head, being, the image and glory of God,—but, the woman, is the glory of man;
text.KJV: 11:7 For a man in deede ought not to couer his head, forasmuch as hee is the image and glory of God: but the woman is the glory of the man.
text.NABRE: 11:7 A man, on the other hand, should not cover his head, because he is the image and glory of God, but woman is the glory of man.

text.NRSV: Indeed, man was not made from woman, but woman from man.
text.Rotherham: For, man, is not, of woman, but, woman, of man;
text.KJV: 11:8 For the man is not of the woman: but the woman of the man.
text.NABRE: 11:8 For man did not come from woman, but woman from man;

text.NRSV: Neither was man created for the sake of woman, but woman for the sake of man.
text.Rotherham: For also man was not created, for the sake of the woman, but woman, for the sake of the man.
text.KJV: 11:9 Neither was the man created for the woman: but the woman for the man.
text.NABRE: 11:9 nor was man created for woman, but woman for man;

text.NRSV: For this reason a woman ought to have a symbol of authority on her head, because of the angels.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, ought the woman to have, permission, upon her head, because of the messengers.
text.KJV: 11:10 For this cause ought the woman to haue power on her head, because of the Angels.
text.NABRE: 11:10 for this reason a woman should have a sign of authority on her head, because of the angels.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless, in the Lord woman is not independent of man or man independent of woman.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, neither is woman apart from man, nor man apart from woman—in the Lord;
text.KJV: 11:11 Neuerthelesse, neither is the man without the woman, neither the woman without the man in the Lord.
text.NABRE: 11:11 Woman is not independent of man or man of woman in the Lord.

text.NRSV: For just as woman came from man, so man comes through woman; but all things come from God.
text.Rotherham: For, just as the woman is of the man, so, also the man, is through means of the woman;—but, all things, are of God.
text.KJV: 11:12 For as the woman is of the man: euen so is the man also by the woman; but all things of God.
text.NABRE: 11:12 For just as woman came from man, so man is born of woman; but all things are from God.

text.NRSV: Judge for yourselves: is it proper for a woman to pray to God with her head unveiled?
text.Rotherham: Among your own selves, judge ye,—Is it becoming for a woman,—unveiled, to be praying unto God?
text.KJV: 11:13 Iudge in your selues, is it comely that a woman pray vnto God vncouered?
text.NABRE: 11:13 Judge for yourselves: is it proper for a woman to pray to God with her head unveiled?

text.NRSV: Does not nature itself teach you that if a man wears his hair long, it is degrading to him,
text.Rotherham: Doth not, even nature herself, teach you—that, if, a man, have long hair, it is a dishonour to him;
text.KJV: 11:14 Doeth not euen nature it selfe teach you, that if a man haue long haire, it is a shame vnto him?
text.NABRE: 11:14 Does not nature itself teach you that if a man wears his hair long it is a disgrace to him,

text.NRSV: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? For her hair is given to her for a covering.
text.Rotherham: But, if, a woman, have long hair, it is a glory to her, for, her long hair, instead of a veil, hath been given to her.
text.KJV: 11:15 But if a woman haue long haire, it is a glory to her: for her haire is giuen her for a couering.
text.NABRE: 11:15 whereas if a woman has long hair it is her glory, because long hair has been given [her] for a covering?

text.NRSV: If anyone is disposed to be contentious—we have no such custom, nor do the churches of God.
text.Rotherham: But, if anyone thinketh to be contentious, we, have no such custom,—nor yet the assemblies of God.
text.KJV: 11:16 But if any man seeme to be contentious, we haue no such custome, neither the Churches of God.
text.NABRE: 11:16 But if anyone is inclined to be argumentative, we do not have such a custom, nor do the churches of God.

text.NRSV: Now in the following instructions I do not commend you, because when you come together it is not for the better but for the worse.
text.Rotherham: But, in giving you the following charge, I praise you not,—in that, not for the better, but for the worse, ye come together.
text.KJV: 11:17 Now in this that I declare vnto you, I praise you not, that you come together not for the better, but for the worse.
text.NABRE: 11:17 B. The Lord’s Supper - An Abuse at Corinth. In giving this instruction, I do not praise the fact that your meetings are doing more harm than good.

text.NRSV: For, to begin with, when you come together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you; and to some extent I believe it.
text.Rotherham: For, first of all,—when ye come together in assembly, I hear that divisions among you exist,—and, in part, I believe it;
text.KJV: 11:18 For first of all when yee come together in the Church, I heare that there be diuisions among you, and I partly beleeue it.
text.NABRE: 11:18 First of all, I hear that when you meet as a church there are divisions among you, and to a degree I believe it;

text.NRSV: Indeed, there have to be factions among you, for only so will it become clear who among you are genuine.
text.Rotherham: For there must needs be, even parties among you, that, the approved, [themselves] may become manifest among you.
text.KJV: 11:19 For there must bee also heresies among you, that they which are approued may be made manifest among you.
text.NABRE: 11:19 there have to be factions among you in order that [also] those who are approved among you may become known.

text.NRSV: When you come together, it is not really to eat the Lord’s supper.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, ye come together into one place, it is not to eat, a supper unto the Lord;
text.KJV: 11:20 When yee come together therefore into one place, this is not to eate the Lords Supper.
text.NABRE: 11:20 When you meet in one place, then, it is not to eat the Lord’s supper,

text.NRSV: For when the time comes to eat, each of you goes ahead with your own supper, and one goes hungry and another becomes drunk.
text.Rotherham: For, each one, taketh beforehand, his own supper, in your eating, and, one, indeed, is hungry, whereas, another, is drinking to excess.
text.KJV: 11:21 For in eating, euery one taketh before other, his owne supper: and one is hungry, and an other is drunken.
text.NABRE: 11:21 for in eating, each one goes ahead with his own supper, and one goes hungry while another gets drunk.

text.NRSV: What! Do you not have homes to eat and drink in? Or do you show contempt for the church of God and humiliate those who have nothing? What should I say to you? Should I commend you? In this matter I do not commend you!
text.Rotherham: What! have ye not houses for your eating and drinking? or, the assembly of God, do ye despise, and, put to shame them who have nothing? What am I to say to you? Shall I praise you? In this, I praise you not.
text.KJV: 11:22 What, haue ye not houses to eate and to drinke in? Or despise yee the Church of God, and shame them that haue not? What shall I say to you? shall I praise you in this? I prayse you not.
text.NABRE: 11:22 Do you not have houses in which you can eat and drink? Or do you show contempt for the church of God and make those who have nothing feel ashamed? What can I say to you? Shall I praise you? In this matter I do not praise you.

text.NRSV: For I received from the Lord what I also handed on to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night when he was betrayed took a loaf of bread,
text.Rotherham: For, I myself, received from the Lord—that which I also delivered unto you,—how that, the Lord Jesus, in the night in which he was being delivered up, took a loaf,
text.KJV: 11:23 For I haue receiued of the Lord that which also I deliuered vnto you, that the Lord Iesus, the same night in which he was betrayed, tooke bread:
text.NABRE: 11:23 Tradition of the Institution. For I received from the Lord what I also handed on to you, that the Lord Jesus, on the night he was handed over, took bread,

text.NRSV: and when he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body that is for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
text.Rotherham: And, giving thanks, brake it, and said—This, is my body, which is for you,—this, do ye, in remembrance of me;
text.KJV: 11:24 And when he had giuen thanks, he brake it, and sayd, Take, eate, this is my body, which is broken for you: this doe in remembrance of mee.
text.NABRE: 11:24 and, after he had given thanks, broke it and said, “This is my body that is for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”

text.NRSV: In the same way he took the cup also, after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of me.”
text.Rotherham: In the same manner, the cup also, after they had taken supper, saying—This cup, is, the new covenant in my blood, this, do ye, as often soever as ye may be drinking it, in remembrance of me.
text.KJV: 11:25 After the same manner also hee tooke the cup when he had supped, saying, This cup is the new Testament in my blood: this do ye, as oft as ye drinke it, in remembrance of me.
text.NABRE: 11:25 In the same way also the cup, after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of me.”

text.NRSV: For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until he comes.
text.Rotherham: For, as often as ye may be eating this loaf, and, the cup, may be drinking, The death of the Lord, do ye announce, until he come.
text.KJV: 11:26 For as often as ye eate this bread, and drinke this cup, yee doe shew the Lords death till he come.
text.NABRE: 11:26 For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the death of the Lord until he comes.

text.NRSV: Whoever, therefore, eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be answerable for the body and blood of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So that, whosoever may be eating the loaf, or drinking the cup of the Lord, in an unworthy manner, shall be responsible for the body and blood of the Lord.
text.KJV: 11:27 Wherefore, whosoeuer shall eate this bread, and drinke this cup of the Lord vnworthily, shall be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 11:27 Therefore whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord unworthily will have to answer for the body and blood of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Examine yourselves, and only then eat of the bread and drink of the cup.
text.Rotherham: But let a man be proving himself,—and, so, of the loaf, let him eat, and, of the cup, let him drink;
text.KJV: 11:28 But let a man examine himselfe, and so let him eate of that bread, and drinke of that cup.
text.NABRE: 11:28 A person should examine himself, and so eat the bread and drink the cup.

text.NRSV: For all who eat and drink without discerning the body, eat and drink judgment against themselves.
text.Rotherham: For, he that is eating and drinking,—judgment unto himself, doth eat and drink,—if he be not setting apart the body.
text.KJV: 11:29 For hee that eateth and drinketh vnworthily, eateth and drinketh damnation to himselfe, not discerning the Lords body.
text.NABRE: 11:29 For anyone who eats and drinks without discerning the body, eats and drinks judgment on himself.

text.NRSV: For this reason many of you are weak and ill, and some have died.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, many among you are weak and sickly; and, not a few, are falling asleep.
text.KJV: 11:30 For this cause many are weake and sickly among you, and many sleepe.
text.NABRE: 11:30 That is why many among you are ill and infirm, and a considerable number are dying.

text.NRSV: But if we judged ourselves, we would not be judged.
text.Rotherham: If, however, we had been setting, ourselves, apart, we had not, in that case, been coming under judgment;
text.KJV: 11:31 For if we would iudge our selues, we should not be iudged.
text.NABRE: 11:31 If we discerned ourselves, we would not be under judgment;

text.NRSV: But when we are judged by the Lord, we are disciplined so that we may not be condemned along with the world.
text.Rotherham: But, being brought under judgment, by the Lord, are we being disciplined, lest, with the world, we should be condemned.
text.KJV: 11:32 But when we are iudged, we are chastened of the Lord, that wee should not be condemned with the world.
text.NABRE: 11:32 but since we are judged by [the] Lord, we are being disciplined so that we may not be condemned along with the world.

text.NRSV: So then, my brothers and sisters, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.
text.Rotherham: So then, my brethren,—when ye come together for the eating, unto one another, be giving welcome:—
text.KJV: 11:33 Wherefore my brethren, when ye come together to eate, tary one for another.
text.NABRE: 11:33 Therefore, my brothers, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.

text.NRSV: If you are hungry, eat at home, so that when you come together, it will not be for your condemnation. About the other things I will give instructions when I come.
text.Rotherham: If anyone be hungry, at home, let him eat, lest, unto judgment, ye be coming together. And, the remaining things, whensoever I come, I will set in order.
text.KJV: 11:34 And if any man hunger, let him eate at home, that ye come not together vnto condemnation. And the rest wil I set in order, when I come.
text.NABRE: 11:34 If anyone is hungry, he should eat at home, so that your meetings may not result in judgment. The other matters I shall set in order when I come.

text.NRSV: Now concerning spiritual gifts, brothers and sisters, I do not want you to be uninformed.
text.Rotherham: But, concerning the spiritually gifted, brethren, I am not wishing you to be ignorant.
text.KJV: 12:1 Now concerning spirituall giftes, brethren, I would not haue you ignorant.
text.NABRE: 12:1 C. Spiritual Gifts Chapter 12 - Unity and Variety. Now in regard to spiritual gifts, brothers, I do not want you to be unaware.

text.NRSV: You know that when you were pagans, you were enticed and led astray to idols that could not speak.
text.Rotherham: Ye know that, when ye were of the nations, unto the dumb idols, howsoever ye were being led, ye were seduced.
text.KJV: 12:2 Yee know that yee were Gentiles, caryed away vnto these dumbe idoles, euen as ye were led.
text.NABRE: 12:2 You know how, when you were pagans, you were constantly attracted and led away to mute idols.

text.NRSV: Therefore I want you to understand that no one speaking by the Spirit of God ever says “Let Jesus be cursed!” and no one can say “Jesus is Lord” except by the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, I give you to know—that, no one, in God’s Spirit speaking, saith—Accursed Jesus! and, no one, can say—Lord Jesus! save in [the] Holy Spirit.
text.KJV: 12:3 Wherefore I giue you to vnderstand, that no man speaking by the spirit of God, calleth Iesus accursed: and that no man can say that Iesus is the Lord, but by the holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: 12:3 Therefore, I tell you that nobody speaking by the spirit of God says, “Jesus be accursed.” And no one can say, “Jesus is Lord,” except by the holy Spirit.

text.NRSV: Now there are varieties of gifts, but the same Spirit;
text.Rotherham: But, distributions of gifts, there are, yet the same Spirit,
text.KJV: 12:4 Nowe there are diuersities of gifts, but the same spirit.
text.NABRE: 12:4 There are different kinds of spiritual gifts but the same Spirit;

text.NRSV: and there are varieties of services, but the same Lord;
text.Rotherham: And, distributions of ministries, there are, and the same Lord,
text.KJV: 12:5 And there are differences of administrations, but the same Lord.
text.NABRE: 12:5 there are different forms of service but the same Lord;

text.NRSV: and there are varieties of activities, but it is the same God who activates all of them in everyone.
text.Rotherham: And, distributions of energies, there are, and the same God—who energiseth all things in all.
text.KJV: 12:6 And there are diuersities of operations, but it is the same God, which worketh all in all.
text.NABRE: 12:6 there are different workings but the same God who produces all of them in everyone.

text.NRSV: To each is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the common good.
text.Rotherham: But, unto each one, is given the manifesting of the Spirit, with a view to that which is profitable;
text.KJV: 12:7 But the manifestation of the spirit, is giuen to euery man to profit withall.
text.NABRE: 12:7 To each individual the manifestation of the Spirit is given for some benefit.

text.NRSV: To one is given through the Spirit the utterance of wisdom, and to another the utterance of knowledge according to the same Spirit,
text.Rotherham: For, unto one, indeed, through the Spirit, is given, a word of wisdom, but, unto another, a word of knowledge, according to the same Spirit;—
text.KJV: 12:8 For to one is giuen by the spirit, the word of wisedome, to another the word of knowledge, by the same spirit.
text.NABRE: 12:8 To one is given through the Spirit the expression of wisdom; to another the expression of knowledge according to the same Spirit;

text.NRSV: to another faith by the same Spirit, to another gifts of healing by the one Spirit,
text.Rotherham: Unto a different one, faith, in the same Spirit, and, unto another, gifts of healings, in the one Spirit,
text.KJV: 12:9 To another faith, by the same spirit: to another the gifts of healing, by the same spirit:
text.NABRE: 12:9 to another faith by the same Spirit; to another gifts of healing by the one Spirit;

text.NRSV: to another the working of miracles, to another prophecy, to another the discernment of spirits, to another various kinds of tongues, to another the interpretation of tongues.
text.Rotherham: And, unto another, energies of mighty works, [and], unto another, prophesying, [and], unto another, discriminations of spirits,—unto a different one, kinds of tongues, and, unto another, translation of tongues;—
text.KJV: 12:10 To another the working of miracles, to another prophecie, to another discerning of spirits, to another diuers kindes of tongues, to another the interpretation of tongues.
text.NABRE: 12:10 to another mighty deeds; to another prophecy; to another discernment of spirits; to another varieties of tongues; to another interpretation of tongues.

text.NRSV: All these are activated by one and the same Spirit, who allots to each one individually just as the Spirit chooses.
text.Rotherham: But, all these, energiseth the one and the same Spirit, distributing unto each one, peculiarly, even as it is disposed.
text.KJV: 12:11 But all these worketh that one and the selfe same spirit, diuiding to euery man seuerally as he will.
text.NABRE: 12:11 But one and the same Spirit produces all of these, distributing them individually to each person as he wishes. One Body, Many Parts.

text.NRSV: For just as the body is one and has many members, and all the members of the body, though many, are one body, so it is with Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, just as, the body, is one, and yet hath many members, but, all the members of the body, though many, are one body, so, also, the Christ;—
text.KJV: 12:12 For as the body is one, and hath many members, and all the membrs of that one body, being many, are one bodie: so also is Christ.
text.NABRE: 12:12 As a body is one though it has many parts, and all the parts of the body, though many, are one body, so also Christ.

text.NRSV: For in the one Spirit we were all baptized into one body—Jews or Greeks, slaves or free—and we were all made to drink of one Spirit.
text.Rotherham: For, even to one Spirit, we all, into one body, have been immersed,—whether Jews or Greeks, whether bond or free,—and, all, of one Spirit, have been caused to drink.
text.KJV: 12:13 For by one spirit are we all baptized into one bodie, whether wee bee Iewes or Gentiles, whether wee bee bond or free: and haue beene all made to drinke into one spirit.
text.NABRE: 12:13 For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body, whether Jews or Greeks, slaves or free persons, and we were all given to drink of one Spirit.

text.NRSV: Indeed, the body does not consist of one member but of many.
text.Rotherham: For, the body also, is not one member, but many:
text.KJV: 12:14 For the body is not one member, but many.
text.NABRE: 12:14 Now the body is not a single part, but many.

text.NRSV: If the foot would say, “Because I am not a hand, I do not belong to the body,” that would not make it any less a part of the body.
text.Rotherham: Though the foot should say—Because I am not a hand, I am not of the body, Is it, therefore, not, of the body?
text.KJV: 12:15 If the foot shall say, Because I am not the hand, I am not of the body: is it therefore not of the body?
text.NABRE: 12:15 If a foot should say, “Because I am not a hand I do not belong to the body,” it does not for this reason belong any less to the body.

text.NRSV: And if the ear would say, “Because I am not an eye, I do not belong to the body,” that would not make it any less a part of the body.
text.Rotherham: And, though the ear should say—Because I am not an eye, I am not of the body, Is it, therefore, not, of the body?
text.KJV: 12:16 And if the eare shall say, Because I am not the eye, I am not of the body: is it therefore not of the body?
text.NABRE: 12:16 Or if an ear should say, “Because I am not an eye I do not belong to the body,” it does not for this reason belong any less to the body.

text.NRSV: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole body were hearing, where would the sense of smell be?
text.Rotherham: If, the whole body, were, an eye, where were the hearing? If, the whole were, hearing, where were, the smelling?
text.KJV: 12:17 If the whole body were an eye, where were the hearing? If the whole were hearing, where were the smelling?
text.NABRE: 12:17 If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole body were hearing, where would the sense of smell be?

text.NRSV: But as it is, God arranged the members in the body, each one of them, as he chose.
text.Rotherham: But, now, hath God set the members, each one of them in the body, even as he pleased.
text.KJV: 12:18 But now hath God set the members, euery one of them in the body, as it hath pleased him.
text.NABRE: 12:18 But as it is, God placed the parts, each one of them, in the body as he intended.

text.NRSV: If all were a single member, where would the body be?
text.Rotherham: But, if they all had been one member, Where had been, the body?
text.KJV: 12:19 And if they were all one member, where were the body?
text.NABRE: 12:19 If they were all one part, where would the body be?

text.NRSV: As it is, there are many members, yet one body.
text.Rotherham: Now, however there are many members, yet one body;
text.KJV: 12:20 But now are they many members, yet but one body.
text.NABRE: 12:20 But as it is, there are many parts, yet one body.

text.NRSV: The eye cannot say to the hand, “I have no need of you,” nor again the head to the feet, “I have no need of you.”
text.Rotherham: [And] the eye cannot say unto the hand—I have no need of thee,—nor, again, the head, unto the feet—I have no need of you:—
text.KJV: 12:21 And the eye cannot say vnto the hand, I haue no need of thee: nor againe, the head to the feete, I haue no neede of you.
text.NABRE: 12:21 The eye cannot say to the hand, “I do not need you,” nor again the head to the feet, “I do not need you.”

text.NRSV: On the contrary, the members of the body that seem to be weaker are indispensable,
text.Rotherham: Nay! much more, the members which seem to be weaker are necessary,
text.KJV: 12:22 Nay, much more those members of the bodie, which seeme to bee more feeble, are necessary.
text.NABRE: 12:22 Indeed, the parts of the body that seem to be weaker are all the more necessary,

text.NRSV: and those members of the body that we think less honorable we clothe with greater honor, and our less respectable members are treated with greater respect;
text.Rotherham: And, the parts of the body which we deem to be less honourable, on these, more abundant honour, do we bestow, and, our uncomely parts, have, more abundant comeliness,
text.KJV: 12:23 And those members of the bodie, which wee thinke to bee lesse honourable, vpon these we bestow more abundant honour, and our vncomely parts haue more abundant comelinesse.
text.NABRE: 12:23 and those parts of the body that we consider less honorable we surround with greater honor, and our less presentable parts are treated with greater propriety,

text.NRSV: whereas our more respectable members do not need this. But God has so constructed the body, giving the greater honor to the inferior member,
text.Rotherham: Whereas, our comely parts, have, no need. But, God, hath tempered the body together,—unto that which was lacking, giving more abundant honour;—
text.KJV: 12:24 For our comely parts haue no need: but God hath tempered the bodie together, hauing giuen more abundant honour to that part which lacked:
text.NABRE: 12:24 whereas our more presentable parts do not need this. But God has so constructed the body as to give greater honor to a part that is without it,

text.NRSV: so that there may be no dissension in the body, but the members may have the same care for one another.
text.Rotherham: That there might be no division in the body, but that the members might have, the same, care, one for another;
text.KJV: 12:25 That there should be no schisme in the body: but that the members should haue the same care one for another.
text.NABRE: 12:25 so that there may be no division in the body, but that the parts may have the same concern for one another.

text.NRSV: If one member suffers, all suffer together with it; if one member is honored, all rejoice together with it.
text.Rotherham: And, whether one member, suffereth, all the members, suffer with it, or, a member is glorified, all the members, rejoice together.
text.KJV: 12:26 And whether one member suffer, all the members suffer with it: or one member be honoured, all the members reioyce with it.
text.NABRE: 12:26 If [one] part suffers, all the parts suffer with it; if one part is honored, all the parts share its joy. Application to Christ.

text.NRSV: Now you are the body of Christ and individually members of it.
text.Rotherham: Now, ye, are the body of Christ, and members severally;—
text.KJV: 12:27 Now yee are the body of Christ, and members in particular.
text.NABRE: 12:27 Now you are Christ’s body, and individually parts of it.

text.NRSV: And God has appointed in the church first apostles, second prophets, third teachers; then deeds of power, then gifts of healing, forms of assistance, forms of leadership, various kinds of tongues.
text.Rotherham: And God hath, indeed, set, certain, in the assembly,—first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, after that mighty works, then gifts of healings, helps, guidings, kinds of tongues.
text.KJV: 12:28 And God hath set some in the Church, first Apostles, secondarily Prophets, thirdly Teachers, after that miracles, then gifts of healings, helpes in gouernmēts, diuersities of tongues.
text.NABRE: 12:28 Some people God has designated in the church to be, first, apostles; second, prophets; third, teachers; then, mighty deeds; then, gifts of healing, assistance, administration, and varieties of tongues.

text.NRSV: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all work miracles?
